summary: Three independent derivation routes for D1-D3 (MI LRT differential bias), each refereed

deriv.D1_bias_formula_H0: At H0, E[d_L − d_com] = tr(RIV_f) − tr[ (Gᵀ I_com G)^{-1} (Gᵀ I_com I_obs^{-1} I_mis G) ] + O(n^{-1}), where RIV_f = I_obs^{-1} I_mis and I_com = I_obs + I_mis. Equivalently, writing the trace differential as tr(I_obs^{-1} I_com) − tr[(Gᵀ I_com G)^{-1}(Gᵀ I_com I_obs^{-1} I_com G)] (the −k and +k0 and −q_d cancel since k − k0 = q_d). The null term is the trace of the SHARED-imputation missing-information ratio for the null model, projected in the COMPLETE-DATA metric I_com (oblique projector P = G(Gᵀ I_com G)^{-1} Gᵀ I_com), NOT the observed-data metric.
deriv.D1_remainder_order: O(n^{-1}). (Leading differential is O(1); both the next deterministic correction and the expectation of the O_p(n^{-1/2}) realized fluctuations enter at O(n^{-1}).)
deriv.D1_vs_naive_diff: No, they do NOT coincide in general. Naive = tr(RIV_f) − tr[(Gᵀ I_obs G)^{-1}(Gᵀ I_mis G)]; the true null term replaces the I_obs-metric projector by the I_com-metric one. EXACT coincidence condition: I_mis = c·I_obs (proportional / equal-fraction-of-missing-information, EFMI), under which both the full and the projected null term reduce to c·(trace of an identity) — true gives c·q_d, naive gives c·q_d, and indeed E[d_L−d_com] = c·q_d exactly. (Also coincide trivially when q_d=0 or G spans an I_obs- and I_mis-simultaneously-invariant subspace, i.e. when GᵀI_com I_obs^{-1}I_mis G aligns with GᵀI_mis G·(GᵀI_obs G)^{-1}GᵀI_com G.) In general the discrepancy = tr[(Gᵀ I_obs G)^{-1}Gᵀ I_mis G] − tr[(Gᵀ I_com G)^{-1}Gᵀ I_com I_obs^{-1}I_mis G]; numerically O(1), sign indefinite (in the verification it was +0.167, i.e. naive over-states the differential because the I_obs-metric self-contained null absorbs more missing information than the shared-imputation constrained fit does). Magnitude is bounded by the spread of the missing-information fractions across the q_d directions removed by the constraint; it vanishes as the EFMI condition is approached.
deriv.D2_sd_order_H0: O(1) (i.e. O(n^{0})). The paired differential D = (d_L − d_com)/2 has bounded standard deviation at H0 — it does NOT inherit the sqrt(n) heavy tail of the individual levels.
deriv.D2_sd_order_local: O(1). Under contiguous local alternatives θ_n = g(γ0)+h/√n the leading O_p(√n) score-noise pieces are still common to both fits and cancel; the noncentrality h enters deterministically (O(1) shift of the mean) plus an O(1)-sd cross term. sd(D) = O(1).
deriv.D2_sd_order_fixed: O(√n). At a fixed alternative the two fits chase pseudo-truths that are O(1) apart, so they no longer share the same linear noise direction; the O_p(√n) fluctuation of the level no longer cancels and sd(D) grows like √n (with the constant set by the O(1) separation of the pseudo-truths / the difference of the two models' realized F-noise loadings).
deriv.D2_mechanism: Both fits maximize ONE common realized objective Q̄(θ)=l_obs(θ)+F(θ,θ̂_obs) (same imputation parameter, same realized F, same realized l_obs). The heavy O_p(√n) noise in a single level T comes from the linear-in-δ term of F(θ0,θ̂_imp): tr-type fluctuation α_Rᵀδ_obs + the realized-minus-expected score, all of which are functions of the SAME (l_obs, F, θ̂_obs). In the difference D = T_f − T_0 the common additive bracket Q̄(θ0)−l_com(θ0) cancels exactly; the realized gradient u = ∇Q̄(θ0)=S+I_{mis,R}δ_obs is the SAME vector entering both max-gains; what survives is the difference of two quadratic forms ½uᵀ[I_com^{-1} − P I_com^{-1}]u minus the complete-data analogue, where P projects onto the constrained tangent space. Since u and the complete-data score S_com are O_p(√n) but the SAME realizations feed both terms, the leading O_p(√n)·O_p(√n)=O_p(n) magnitudes cancel and the surviving quadratic form fluctuates at O_p(1). At H0 the projected-out directions carry only q_d-dimensional χ²-type noise (O_p(1)); under local alternatives a deterministic noncentrality adds an O(1) mean with O(1) sd; only at a fixed alternative do the two objectives' noise directions decouple, reviving the √n tail.
deriv.D3_AC_diff_H0: At H0 the (A)+(C) differential is EXACTLY zero at leading order, with first nonvanishing contribution O(n^{-1}). Mechanism: (A)+(C) for a model = αᵀE[δ] + ½ tr(H_φ Var(δ)); α and H_φ live only on the covariance coordinates and are properties of the SHARED imputation (full-model θ̂_imp=θ̂_obs, same α_R, H_R for both fits). At H0 the two fits' relevant deltas, δ_f = ψ̂−θ0 and δ_0 = g(γ̂*)−θ0, satisfy δ_f − δ_0 = (I−P)I_com^{-1}u = O_p(n^{-1/2}) with E[δ_f]−E[δ_0] = O(n^{-1}) (both are unbiased to O(n^{-1}); the projection (I−P) is a fixed O(1) operator). Hence αᵀ(E[δ_f]−E[δ_0]) = O(n)·O(n^{-1}) = O(1)·O(n^{-1}) → O(n^{-1}) after the cross-term bookkeeping; more precisely the α-term and the H_φ-term differential are both O(n^{-1}) because α=O(n), E[δ]=O(n^{-1}) give an O(1) level per model but the DIFFERENCE across the common-α models is governed by E[δ_f−δ_0]=O(n^{-1}) and Var(δ_f)−Var(δ_0)=O(n^{-1}), and α,H_φ are common. So the (A)+(C) pieces cancel to O(1) and differ only at O(n^{-1}).
deriv.D3_AC_diff_local: o(1): under local alternatives θ_n=g(γ0)+h/√n the (A)+(C) differential is o(1) — specifically O(n^{-1/2}). The local shift moves E[δ_0] by an O(n^{-1/2}) amount relative to E[δ_f] in the projected-out directions (the q_d directions where h has a component outside col(G)), so αᵀ(E[δ_f]−E[δ_0]) = O(n)·O(n^{-1/2})·(coordinate overlap) — but α is supported only on covariance coordinates while the local shift h is a generic location/scale perturbation, so the overlap is O(n^{-1/2}) in the level differential, i.e. O(n^{-1/2})→o(1). Therefore the information-trace differential tr(RIV_f) − tr[(GᵀI_com G)^{-1}GᵀI_com I_obs^{-1}I_mis G] (the O(1) D1 term, which acquires an additional O(1) noncentrality-weighted piece locally) DOMINATES the local power distortion; the (A)+(C) differential is asymptotically negligible for local power. At a FIXED alternative the pseudo-truths are O(1) apart, α and H_φ are evaluated at two different O(1)-separated points (the two pseudo-truths), so the (A)+(C) pieces no longer share common α/H_φ and their differential is a genuine O(1) quantity — it contributes at the same order as the trace differential to non-nested model comparison.
deriv.derivation_md:
## Differential bias of the MI deviance numerator: a from-scratch derivation via the difference of levels

### 0. Setup, notation, and the one fact that organizes everything

We have $Y_i\sim N_p(\mu,\Sigma)$, $\theta=(\mu,\operatorname{vech}\Sigma)\in\mathbb R^k$, MAR-ignorable missingness with a nuisance mechanism parameter. Write $S=\nabla \ell_{\rm obs}(\theta_0)$ (observed-data score), $\delta_{\rm obs}=\hat\theta_{\rm obs}-\theta_0$. Standard FIML MLE asymptotics (which I use throughout, naming them **(MLE-mom)**):
$$E S=0,\quad \operatorname{Var}S=I_{\rm obs}=O(n),\quad \delta_{\rm obs}=I_{\rm obs}^{-1}S+O_p(n^{-1}),\quad E\delta_{\rm obs}=O(n^{-1}),\ \operatorname{Var}\delta_{\rm obs}=I_{\rm obs}^{-1}=O(n^{-1}),$$
signed third/fourth central moments $O(n^{-2})$. $I_{\rm com}=I_{\rm obs}+I_{\rm mis}$ (Orchard–Woodbury), $\mathrm{RIV}_f=I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm mis}$, $\operatorname{tr}\mathrm{RIV}_f=O(1)$.

The **single organizing identity** is the MI/EM completion identity for the realized objective. Since $\log p_\psi(Y_{\rm com})=\log p_\psi(Y_{\rm obs})+\log p_\psi(Y_{\rm mis}\mid Y_{\rm obs})$, taking the conditional expectation of $Y_{\rm mis}$ under $p_\phi(\cdot\mid Y_{\rm obs})$ gives
$$\boxed{\;\bar Q(\theta)=\ell_{\rm obs}(\theta)+F(\theta,\hat\theta_{\rm imp})=\sum_i E_{Y_{\rm mis,i}\sim p_{\hat\theta_{\rm imp}}}\big[\log p_\theta(Y_{{\rm com},i})\big]\;}$$
i.e. **$\bar Q$ is the imputed complete-data log-likelihood**. This is what makes the difference-of-levels route tractable, because both model fits are M-estimators of this *one* realized function.

### 1. Curvature and gradient of $\bar Q$ at $\theta_0$ (where I use F1–F3)

**Gradient.** $\nabla\bar Q(\theta_0)=S+\nabla_\psi F(\theta_0,\hat\theta_{\rm imp})$. By **(F1)**, $\nabla_\psi F(\theta_0,\theta_0)=0$ exactly for every realization. Taylor-expand in the imputation argument around $\phi=\theta_0$, with $\hat\theta_{\rm imp}=\hat\theta_{\rm obs}$ so $\hat\theta_{\rm imp}-\theta_0=\delta_{\rm obs}$:
$$\nabla_\psi F(\theta_0,\hat\theta_{\rm imp})=\underbrace{\nabla_\psi F(\theta_0,\theta_0)}_{=0\ (\mathrm{F1})}+\underbrace{[\nabla_\psi\nabla_\phi F(\theta_0,\theta_0)]}_{=:I_{\rm mis,R}\ (\mathrm{F2}),\ E=I_{\rm mis}}\delta_{\rm obs}+O_p(n\cdot|\delta_{\rm obs}|^2).$$
The remainder is $O(n)\cdot O_p(n^{-1})=O_p(1)$. Hence the **realized gradient**
$$u:=\nabla\bar Q(\theta_0)=S+I_{\rm mis,R}\,\delta_{\rm obs}+O_p(1).$$
Substituting $\delta_{\rm obs}=I_{\rm obs}^{-1}S+O_p(n^{-1})$ and using $I_{\rm com}=I_{\rm obs}+I_{\rm mis}$:
$$u=\big(I+I_{\rm mis}I_{\rm obs}^{-1}\big)S+O_p(1)=I_{\rm com}I_{\rm obs}^{-1}S+O_p(1),\qquad \operatorname{Var}u=I_{\rm com}I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm com}=:V_u=O(n).$$

**Curvature.** Because $\bar Q$ *is* the imputed complete log-likelihood, its Hessian is the imputed complete-data Hessian:
$$\nabla^2\bar Q(\theta_0)=\nabla^2\ell_{\rm obs}(\theta_0)+\nabla_\psi^2F(\theta_0,\theta_0).$$
$\nabla_\psi^2 F(\theta_0,\theta_0)$ is the conditional-density Hessian; by the Bartlett identity for $p_\psi(Y_{\rm mis}\mid Y_{\rm obs})$ its expectation is $-I_{\rm mis}$ (realized $-I_{\rm mis,R}$, the same object as in **(F2)** by Louis' identity). With $E[-\nabla^2\ell_{\rm obs}]=I_{\rm obs}$:
$$-E\,\nabla^2\bar Q(\theta_0)=I_{\rm obs}+I_{\rm mis}=I_{\rm com}.$$
So **$\bar Q$ curves like complete data**: $A:=-E\,\nabla^2\bar Q=I_{\rm com}$. This is the single most important structural fact for the route. (The realized curvature equals $-I_{\rm com}+O_p(n^{1/2})$ by **(F3)**; the $O_p(n^{1/2})$ fluctuation feeds the per-dataset noise, not the mean.)

### 2. Where the two fits sit (which projection, which metric)

**Full fit.** $\hat\psi$ solves $\nabla\bar Q(\hat\psi)=0$. A one-step expansion using $A=I_{\rm com}$:
$$\hat\psi-\theta_0=I_{\rm com}^{-1}u+O_p(n^{-1}).$$

**Null fit on the SHARED imputation.** $\hat\gamma^*$ maximizes $\bar Q(g(\gamma))$, so it solves the constrained estimating equation $G^\top\nabla\bar Q(g(\hat\gamma^*))=0$ with $G=dg/d\gamma^\top$. Expanding:
$$0=G^\top\big[u-I_{\rm com}\,(g(\hat\gamma^*)-\theta_0)\big]+O_p(n^{-1})\ \Rightarrow\ G^\top I_{\rm com}G(\hat\gamma^*-\gamma_0)=G^\top u+O_p(n^{-1}),$$
$$g(\hat\gamma^*)-\theta_0=G(G^\top I_{\rm com}G)^{-1}G^\top u+O_p(n^{-1})=:P\,I_{\rm com}^{-1}u,$$
where
$$\boxed{\,P=G(G^\top I_{\rm com}G)^{-1}G^\top I_{\rm com}\,}$$
is the **oblique projector onto $\operatorname{col}(G)$ that is self-adjoint in the $I_{\rm com}$ inner product** ($P^2=P$, $I_{\rm com}P=P^\top I_{\rm com}$). **This is the crux of the route:** because $\bar Q$ curves like $I_{\rm com}$, the shared-imputation constrained fit projects in the **complete-data metric** $I_{\rm com}$ — *not* in the observed-data metric $I_{\rm obs}$ that the null model's self-contained fit would use. The full and null fits differ only through the fixed $O(1)$ operator $(I-P)$ acting on the *same* realized $u$.

### 3. Assembling the two levels $T_f,T_0$ and the differential $D$

Each level is $T_\bullet=\bar Q(\text{fit}_\bullet)-\ell_{\rm com}(\text{fit}^{\rm com}_\bullet)$. Write each as the maximized gain over a baseline plus a common offset:
$$\bar Q(\text{fit})-\ell_{\rm com}(\text{fit}^{\rm com})=\big[\bar Q(\text{fit})-\bar Q(\theta_0)\big]-\big[\ell_{\rm com}(\text{fit}^{\rm com})-\ell_{\rm com}(\theta_0)\big]+\underbrace{\big[\bar Q(\theta_0)-\ell_{\rm com}(\theta_0)\big]}_{=:B,\ \text{common offset}}.$$
The offset $B=F(\theta_0,\hat\theta_{\rm imp})-\log p(Y_{\rm mis}\mid Y_{\rm obs};\theta_0)$ depends **only on $\theta_0$ and the shared imputation** — it is *identical* in $T_f$ and $T_0$. Therefore **$B$ cancels exactly in $D=T_f-T_0$.** (This single cancellation is also why the heavy $\sqrt n$ tail dies; see §5.) This is the precise answer to "what is common and cancels": the realized $l_{\rm obs}$, the realized $F$, the imputation parameter $\hat\theta_{\rm imp}$, the gradient $u$, and the offset $B$ are *all shared*; only the projector $P$ vs $I$ differs.

**Quadratic max-gains** (second-order Taylor at the respective curvatures, $A_{\bar Q}=I_{\rm com}$, $A_{\rm com}=I_{\rm com}$):
$$\bar Q(\hat\psi)-\bar Q(\theta_0)=\tfrac12 u^\top I_{\rm com}^{-1}u,\qquad \bar Q(g(\hat\gamma^*))-\bar Q(\theta_0)=\tfrac12 u^\top P I_{\rm com}^{-1}u=\tfrac12 u^\top G(G^\top I_{\rm com}G)^{-1}G^\top u.$$
$$\ell_{\rm com}(\hat\theta_{\rm com})-\ell_{\rm com}(\theta_0)=\tfrac12 S_{\rm com}^\top I_{\rm com}^{-1}S_{\rm com},\quad \ell_{\rm com}(g(\hat\gamma_{\rm com}))-\ell_{\rm com}(\theta_0)=\tfrac12 S_{\rm com}^\top G(G^\top I_{\rm com}G)^{-1}G^\top S_{\rm com},$$
with $\operatorname{Var}S_{\rm com}=I_{\rm com}$, so the complete-data gains have expectations $\tfrac12 k$ and $\tfrac12 k_0$, and their difference is $\tfrac12 q_d$ (Wilks: $E\,d_{\rm com}=q_d$).

Taking expectations with $E\,u u^\top=V_u=I_{\rm com}I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm com}$ (the $O_p(1)$ remainders in $u$ contribute only at $O(n^{-1})$ to the trace, by **(MLE-mom)** moment orders):
$$E\big[\bar Q(\hat\psi)-\bar Q(\theta_0)\big]=\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(I_{\rm com}^{-1}V_u)=\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm com})=\tfrac12\big(k+\operatorname{tr}\mathrm{RIV}_f\big),$$
$$E\big[\bar Q(g(\hat\gamma^*))-\bar Q(\theta_0)\big]=\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}\!\big[(G^\top I_{\rm com}G)^{-1}G^\top V_u G\big]=\tfrac12\Big(k_0+\operatorname{tr}\!\big[(G^\top I_{\rm com}G)^{-1}G^\top I_{\rm com}I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm mis}G\big]\Big),$$
using $G^\top V_u G=G^\top I_{\rm com}I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm com}G=G^\top I_{\rm com}G+G^\top I_{\rm com}I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm mis}G$.

**Cross-check (G = identity).** With $G=I$, the null term equals $\tfrac12(k+\operatorname{tr}\mathrm{RIV}_f)$, the differential collapses, and the full level reduces to $E[\bar Q(\hat\psi)-\ell_{\rm com}(\hat\theta_{\rm com})]=\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}\mathrm{RIV}_f+[(A)+(C)]$ — **exactly the locked level theorem**. ✓ (Numerically verified: $\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm com})-\tfrac12 k=\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}\mathrm{RIV}_f$ to machine precision.)

### 4. D1 — the differential bias

Subtract. The common offset $B$ cancels; the full gains $\tfrac12 k$, $\tfrac12 k_0$ and the $-q_d$ from $E\,d_{\rm com}=q_d$ combine via $k-k_0-q_d=0$:
$$\boxed{\;E[d_L-d_{\rm com}]=2E[T_f-T_0]=\operatorname{tr}\mathrm{RIV}_f-\operatorname{tr}\!\Big[(G^\top I_{\rm com}G)^{-1}\,G^\top I_{\rm com}I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm mis}\,G\Big]+O(n^{-1}).\;}$$
The remainder is $O(n^{-1})$: it collects the cubic Taylor term (third derivative $O(n)$ times $|\hat\psi-\theta_0|^3=O_p(n^{-3/2})$, expectation $O(n^{-1/2})\cdot O(n^{-1/2})$ correlation $\to O(n^{-1})$), the $E\,u=O(1)$ bias term ($O(1)$ into a $\tfrac1n$-scaled quadratic), and the $(A)+(C)$ differential (shown $O(n^{-1})$ in §6).

**Interpretation.** The full model contributes $\operatorname{tr}\mathrm{RIV}_f$; the null model contributes a trace of its missing-information ratio **projected in the complete-data metric $I_{\rm com}$**. At $H_0$ this is a genuine $O(1)$ residual differential (it is $0$ only when $q_d=0$ or under EFMI, below).

#### Comparison with the naive prediction $\operatorname{tr}\mathrm{RIV}_f-\operatorname{tr}\mathrm{RIV}_0$
The naive null term is $\operatorname{tr}\mathrm{RIV}_0=\operatorname{tr}[(G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}(G^\top I_{\rm mis}G)]$ (self-contained: impute from the null's **own** $\hat\theta_{\rm obs}$, project in **$I_{\rm obs}$**). The true null term is $\operatorname{tr}[(G^\top I_{\rm com}G)^{-1}G^\top I_{\rm com}I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm mis}G]$. **They differ** because (a) the shared imputation parameter is the *full* model's MLE, and (b) $\hat\gamma^*$ maximizes the shared $\bar Q$ (curving like $I_{\rm com}$), so the metric is $I_{\rm com}$, not $I_{\rm obs}$.

**Exact coincidence condition.** If $I_{\rm mis}=c\,I_{\rm obs}$ (proportional information / equal-fraction-of-missing-information, EFMI), then $I_{\rm com}=(1+c)I_{\rm obs}$, $I_{\rm com}I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm mis}=c(1+c)I_{\rm obs}$, and
$$\text{true null term}=\operatorname{tr}[(G^\top I_{\rm com}G)^{-1}c(1+c)G^\top I_{\rm obs}G]=c(1+c)\cdot\tfrac{1}{1+c}\,k_0=c\,k_0=\operatorname{tr}\mathrm{RIV}_0,$$
so true $=$ naive $=c\,q_d=E[d_L-d_{\rm com}]$. (Verified numerically: with $c=0.37,q_d=2$, both give $0.74$.) More generally they coincide whenever $G^\top I_{\rm com}I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm mis}G$ and $G^\top I_{\rm mis}G\,(G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}G^\top I_{\rm com}G$ have equal trace — e.g. when $\operatorname{col}(G)$ is invariant under $I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm mis}$.

**Sign/magnitude of the discrepancy.** In general $\text{naive}-\text{true}=\operatorname{tr}[(G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}G^\top I_{\rm mis}G]-\operatorname{tr}[(G^\top I_{\rm com}G)^{-1}G^\top I_{\rm com}I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm mis}G]$, an $O(1)$ quantity of **indefinite sign** controlled by the dispersion of the missing-information fractions across the $q_d$ constrained directions; it $\to 0$ as EFMI is approached. (In the generic verification, naive $=2.447$ vs true $=2.281$: naive **over**-states the differential by $0.167$ — the $I_{\rm obs}$-metric self-contained null absorbs more apparent missing information than the shared-imputation constrained fit.)

### 5. D2 — order of the paired differential's noise

A single level $T_f$ has $\operatorname{sd}=c\sqrt n$ because its dominant fluctuation is the **linear-in-$\delta$** part of $F(\theta_0,\hat\theta_{\rm imp})$: terms like $\alpha_R^\top\delta_{\rm obs}$ and the realized-minus-expected score/info pieces (**F3**), all $O_p(\sqrt n)$, all functions of the shared $(\ell_{\rm obs},F,\hat\theta_{\rm obs})$.

**At $H_0$.** In $D=T_f-T_0$ the common offset $B$ (which contains the entire $O_p(\sqrt n)$ linear-in-$\delta$ fluctuation, since $B$ does not depend on which fit) cancels **exactly** — it is literally the same realized number in both levels. The surviving randomness is the difference of the two quadratic max-gains, both built from the **same** realized $u$ and **same** $S_{\rm com}$:
$$D=\tfrac12 u^\top(I-P)^\top I_{\rm com}^{-1}(I-P)\,u\ \big|_{\text{schematically}}-\tfrac12 S_{\rm com}^\top(I-P)^\top I_{\rm com}^{-1}(I-P)S_{\rm com}+O_p(n^{-1/2}).$$
Each is a quadratic form of a fixed-rank-$q_d$ projection of an $O_p(\sqrt n)$ vector divided by $I_{\rm com}=O(n)$, i.e. a $\chi^2_{q_d}$-type object: $\operatorname{mean}=O(1)$, $\operatorname{sd}=O(1)$. Hence
$$\boxed{\operatorname{sd}(D)=O(1)\quad\text{at }H_0.}$$
The mechanism: the $O_p(\sqrt n)\times O_p(\sqrt n)=O_p(n)$ raw magnitude of $\tfrac12 u^\top I_{\rm com}^{-1}u$ is **common** to $T_f$ and the full part, and the projection $(I-P)$ confines the surviving piece to the $q_d$-dimensional complement, leaving an $O_p(1)$ remainder.

**Local alternatives $\theta_n=g(\gamma_0)+h/\sqrt n$, $h\notin\operatorname{col}(G)$ (contiguity).** Under contiguity the score acquires a deterministic mean $I_{\rm com}^{1/2}$-noncentrality $\propto h$, but the noise vectors $u,S_{\rm com}$ are still shared between the two fits and the offset $B$ still cancels. $D$ becomes a *noncentral* $\chi^2_{q_d}$-type difference: mean shifts by an $O(1)$ noncentrality, sd stays $O(1)$ (the $h$-cross term is $O(1)\times O_p(1)$). So $\boxed{\operatorname{sd}(D)=O(1)}$.

**Fixed alternative (pseudo-truths $O(1)$ apart).** Now the full and null fits converge to **different** $O(1)$-separated pseudo-truths $\theta^*_f\neq\theta^*_0$. Their objectives' linearizations are taken at different points, so the realized $F$-noise loadings $\alpha_R^\top\delta$ no longer coincide: the offset/linear pieces **do not cancel**, the $O_p(\sqrt n)$ tail survives, and $\boxed{\operatorname{sd}(D)=O(\sqrt n)}$, with constant set by the $O(1)$ separation of the pseudo-truths (the difference of the two models' $\alpha$-loadings). This is the regime of non-nested comparison.

### 6. D3 — the $(A)+(C)$ differential

For one model the MAR design-imbalance term is $(A)+(C)=\alpha^\top E[\delta]+\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(H_\phi\operatorname{Var}\delta)+O(n^{-1})$, with $\alpha=O(n)$, $H_\phi=O(n)$ supported on covariance coordinates (**F3**), $E\delta=O(n^{-1})$, $\operatorname{Var}\delta=O(n^{-1})$, giving $O(1)$ per model.

**Shared-imputation key:** $\alpha,H_\phi$ are properties of the **shared** imputation ($\hat\theta_{\rm imp}=\hat\theta_{\rm obs}$, same realized $\alpha_R,H_R$) — **identical operators for both fits**. So the differential is governed entirely by the difference of the fits' moments:
$$[(A)+(C)]_f-[(A)+(C)]_0=\alpha^\top\big(E\delta_f-E\delta_0\big)+\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}\!\big[H_\phi(\operatorname{Var}\delta_f-\operatorname{Var}\delta_0)\big]+O(n^{-1}).$$

**At $H_0$.** $\delta_f-\delta_0=(I-P)I_{\rm com}^{-1}u$ with $(I-P)$ a fixed $O(1)$ operator; both $E\delta_f$ and $E\delta_0$ are $O(n^{-1})$ (unbiased to that order), and they differ by $(I-P)$ acting on the common $O(n^{-1})$ bias of $u$, so $E\delta_f-E\delta_0=O(n^{-1})$. Likewise $\operatorname{Var}\delta_f-\operatorname{Var}\delta_0=O(n^{-1})$ (both $O(n^{-1})$, differing by a fixed projection). Therefore
$$\alpha^\top(E\delta_f-E\delta_0)=O(n)\cdot O(n^{-1})=O(1)?\ \text{— no: the leading }O(n^{-1})\text{ bias is }\textbf{common}\text{ (same }u),$$
so it cancels under $(I-P)$ except at the next order, giving $\alpha^\top(E\delta_f-E\delta_0)=O(n^{-1})$; identically $\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}[H_\phi(\operatorname{Var}\delta_f-\operatorname{Var}\delta_0)]=O(n)\cdot O(n^{-2})=O(n^{-1})$. Hence
$$\boxed{[(A)+(C)]_f-[(A)+(C)]_0=O(n^{-1})\quad\text{at }H_0,\ \text{exactly zero at the }O(1)\text{ (leading) order.}}$$
First nonvanishing contribution: $O(n^{-1})$. So $(A)+(C)$ does **not** contribute to the $O(1)$ D1 differential at $H_0$ — consistent with the differential being purely the information-trace difference there.

**Local alternatives $\theta_n=g(\gamma_0)+h/\sqrt n$.** The local shift perturbs $E\delta_0$ relative to $E\delta_f$ in the $q_d$ projected-out directions by $O(n^{-1/2})$ (the $(I-P)$ image of $h/\sqrt n$). Then $\alpha^\top(E\delta_f-E\delta_0)$ is at most $O(n)\cdot O(n^{-1/2})\cdot(\text{coordinate overlap of }\alpha\text{ with the shift})$. Because $\alpha$ is supported only on covariance coordinates while a generic $h$ excites both location and scale, the level differential is $O(n^{-1/2})=o(1)$. Therefore
$$\boxed{[(A)+(C)]_f-[(A)+(C)]_0=o(1)\ (\text{order }O(n^{-1/2}))\ \text{under local alternatives};}$$
it is asymptotically negligible for **local power**, which is governed instead by the $O(1)$ information-trace differential of D1 (augmented by the noncentrality). So the trace differential **dominates** local power distortion.

**Fixed alternative / non-nested.** Pseudo-truths are $O(1)$ apart, $\alpha,H_\phi$ are evaluated at the two distinct pseudo-truths, the operators are no longer common, and $[(A)+(C)]_f-[(A)+(C)]_0$ is a genuine **$O(1)$** quantity, entering at the same order as the (now $O(1)$, possibly $O(n)$-scaled by separation) trace differential. For non-nested model comparison the $(A)+(C)$ differential **does** matter at leading order.

### 7. Summary of orders

| Quantity | $H_0$ | Local ($h/\sqrt n$) | Fixed alt. |
|---|---|---|---|
| $E[d_L-d_{\rm com}]$ (D1) | $\operatorname{tr}\mathrm{RIV}_f-\operatorname{tr}[(G^\top I_{\rm com}G)^{-1}G^\top I_{\rm com}I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm mis}G]=O(1)$ | $O(1)$ (+ noncentrality) | $O(1)$/$O(n)$-scaled |
| remainder | $O(n^{-1})$ | — | — |
| $\operatorname{sd}(D)$ (D2) | $O(1)$ | $O(1)$ | $O(\sqrt n)$ |
| $(A)+(C)$ differential (D3) | $O(n^{-1})$ (zero at leading order) | $O(n^{-1/2})=o(1)$ | $O(1)$ |

All numerical claims (the closed form, the $G=I$ reduction to $\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}\mathrm{RIV}_f$, the EFMI coincidence $c\,q_d$, and the generic naive-vs-true discrepancy) were verified by direct matrix computation on random SPD $I_{\rm obs},I_{\rm mis}$ and random $G$.

referee.recomputed: I independently recomputed every load-bearing identity. (1) D1 boxed formula E[d_L-d_com] = tr(RIV_f) - tr[(G'I_com G)^{-1} G'I_com I_obs^{-1} I_mis G] + O(1/n): verified two ways - direct trace algebra AND a 400,000-draw Monte Carlo of the actual quadratic forms u'I_com^{-1}u and u'G(G'I_com G)^{-1}G'u with u~N(0, I_com I_obs^{-1} I_com); MC gave E[d_L-d_com]=2.3337 vs boxed=2.3337. SOUND. (2) Projector P=G(G'I_com G)^{-1}G'I_com: confirmed P^2=P, I_com P symmetric (I_com-self-adjoint), PG=G. SOUND. (3) Var(u)=I_com I_obs^{-1} I_com and full-gain E=1/2 tr(I_obs^{-1}I_com)=1/2(k+trRIV_f): verified. (4) tr(I_obs^{-1}I_com)-k=trRIV_f: machine precision. (5) EFMI (I_mis=c I_obs): true null term, naive null term, and D1 all collapse to c*q_d (got 0.74 at c=0.37,q_d=2). SOUND. (6) Generic naive-vs-true discrepancy: confirmed O(1), indefinite sign (got -0.39 and +0.167 in two seeds). (7) Deviance quadratic-form identity: confirmed the correct form is I_com^{-1}-G(G'I_com G)^{-1}G' = (I-P)I_com^{-1} (symmetric), NOT (I-P)'I_com^{-1}(I-P) as §5 writes - the latter gives a different scalar. (8) Headline 'alt representation' tr(I_obs^{-1}I_com)-tr[(G'I_comG)^{-1}G'I_com I_obs^{-1}I_com G]: numerically equals D1+q_d, NOT D1.

issues.description: HEADLINE ARITHMETIC ERROR (does not affect the boxed D1). The D1 headline claims an 'equivalent' rewriting: 'tr(I_obs^{-1} I_com) - tr[(G'I_com G)^{-1}(G'I_com I_obs^{-1} I_com G)] (the -k and +k0 and -q_d cancel since k-k0=q_d)'. This is wrong. The first term = k+trRIV_f; the second term = k0+null_term (verified: G'I_com I_obs^{-1} I_com G = G'I_com G + G'I_com I_obs^{-1} I_mis G, so the trace = k0 + null_term). Their difference = (k+trRIV_f)-(k0+null_term) = trRIV_f - null_term + (k-k0) = D1 + q_d, NOT D1. Numerically the 'alt rep' gave 4.45 vs boxed D1 = 2.45 (off by q_d=2). The cancellation 'k-k0-q_d=0' is mis-stated: in the alt rep there is no -q_d to cancel the +(k-k0). The boxed §4 formula and the §3->§4 derivation via E[d_com]=q_d are CORRECT; only this one-line headline restatement is wrong.

D3 MECHANISM IS MUDDLED / ORDER STEP IS A NON-SEQUITUR (conclusion direction is right, even conservative). §6 derives the (A)+(C) differential at H0 by writing it as alpha^T(E[delta_f]-E[delta_0]) + (1/2)tr[H_phi(Var delta_f - Var delta_0)], where delta_f=psihat-theta0, delta_0=g(gammahat*)-theta0 are the FITS' errors. This conflates the fit's delta with the IMPUTATION error: per the locked theorem, (A)+(C)=alpha^T E[delta_obs] + (1/2)tr(H_phi Var delta_obs) uses delta_OBS = thetahat_obs-theta0 (the SHARED imputation parameter's error), NOT the fit's delta. Since both fits use the SAME imputation (theta_imp=thetahat_obs), alpha, H_phi, AND delta_obs are all identical for both fits, so (A)+(C)_f - (A)+(C)_0 = 0 EXACTLY at H0 (equivalently, (A)+(C) sits entirely in the fit-independent offset B = Qbar(theta0)-l_com(theta0), which the route itself notes cancels exactly in D). Separately, the route's order step 'alpha^T(E[delta_f]-E[delta_0]) = O(n)*O(1/n) = O(1/n)' is a non-sequitur: O(n)*O(1/n)=O(1), not O(1/n); I verified alpha^T(I-P)I_com^{-1}E[u] is a generically nonzero O(1) coefficient (got 0.108), so the delta-based bookkeeping does NOT give O(1/n) without the orthogonality/B-identity the route fails to invoke. Net: the CLAIM ((A)+(C) differential negligible at H0, dominated by trace differential locally) is correct and the true answer (exact 0 under shared imputations) is even stronger; but the stated derivation is unsound as written.

D2 §5 SCHEMATIC FORM GIVES THE WRONG SCALAR (flagged 'schematically' by the author; conclusions unaffected). The §5 expression D = (1/2)u'(I-P)'I_com^{-1}(I-P)u - (1/2)S_com'(I-P)'I_com^{-1}(I-P)S_com is not the correct deviance quadratic form. The actual d_L/2 = (1/2)u'[I_com^{-1}-G(G'I_com G)^{-1}G']u = (1/2)u'(I-P)I_com^{-1}u (symmetric); the doubly-projected (I-P)'I_com^{-1}(I-P) gives a different scalar (verified: 0.353 vs 0.242 on a random u). The §3 expectation computations use the CORRECT trace forms, so the sd(D)=O(1) conclusion stands. Additionally the §5 'mechanism' narrative is a mischaracterization: it claims the O_p(sqrt n) tails of u and S_com are 'identical realizations' that cancel, but u (built from the observed score S, Var=I_obs) and S_com (complete-data score, Var=I_com) are DIFFERENT correlated vectors, not identical. The real reason sd(D)=O(1) is simpler and correct: d_L/2 and d_com/2 are each already chi^2_{q_d}-type with O(1) sd individually (the sqrt(n) tail of the LEVELS lived only in the fit-independent offset B, removed tautologically by D=(d_L-d_com)/2), so their difference is O(1)-sd by subadditivity - no exact sqrt(n) cancellation between u and S_com is needed or occurs.

referee.notes: CORE RESULTS ARE SOUND. The headline D1 formula, the I_com-metric projection insight (the central correct idea: the shared-imputation constrained fit gamma-hat* projects in the COMPLETE-data metric I_com, not I_obs, distinct from the null model's own observed-data MLE), the naive-vs-true discrepancy and its O(1) indefinite-sign characterization, the EFMI coincidence condition (c*q_d), the G=I reduction to (1/2)tr(RIV_f), and all three sd(D) orders (O(1)/O(1)/O(sqrt n)) are CORRECT and I reproduced them numerically. On the referee's specific suspicions: (i) exact cancellations - the offset B cancellation is real but tautological given D=(d_L-d_com)/2; the claimed (A)+(C) exact cancellation at H0 is actually TRUE (stronger than the route's O(1/n)) but for the right reason (shared imputation error delta_obs), not the route's delta_f-vs-delta_0 reason. (ii) order of var-of-quadratic-form/cross terms - the D2 noise orders are right; the §5 mechanism story is wrong but the conclusion holds. (iii) projection metric - CORRECTLY identified as I_com (= -E grad^2 Qbar), the paper's key contribution, properly distinguished from the I_obs metric of the self-contained null; this is exactly right and well-argued. (iv) dropped remainders - the O(1/n) D1 remainder is fine; the D3 O(1/n) order claim is mis-derived (see issues). (v) conflation of gamma-hat* with the null's observed-data MLE - the route does NOT conflate them in D1 (the whole naive-vs-true discussion turns on keeping them distinct, done correctly); the ONLY conflation is the unrelated fit-delta-vs-imputation-delta slip in D3. All three issues are minor: two are headline/exposition errors (one arithmetic slip in a restatement, one explicitly-flagged 'schematic' form) and one is a muddled-but-conservative D3 mechanism whose stated conclusion is correct. None overturns the deliverable's headline answers. Recommend: fix the headline alt-rep (drop the +q_d / use the boxed form directly), restate D3 via the shared-imputation delta_obs / offset-B argument (yielding exact 0 at H0 and local), and correct the §5 quadratic form to (I-P)I_com^{-1}.

deriv.D1_bias_formula_H0: At H0, E[d_L - d_com] = tr(RIV_f) - B + O(n^{-1}), where tr(RIV_f) = tr(I_obs^{-1} I_com) - k = tr(I_obs^{-1} I_mis), and B := tr( (G^T I_com G)^{-1} (G^T I_com I_obs^{-1} I_com G) ) - k_0. Equivalently, with the J-metric between-model projector P := I_com^{-1} - G(G^T I_com G)^{-1} G^T and Var(Z) = I_com I_obs^{-1} I_com: E[d_L] = tr(P · I_com I_obs^{-1} I_com) = tr(I_obs^{-1} I_com) - tr((G^T I_com G)^{-1} G^T I_com I_obs^{-1} I_com G), while E[d_com] = tr(P · I_com) = k - k_0 = q_d; subtracting gives E[d_L - d_com] = [tr(I_obs^{-1} I_com) - k] - [tr((G^T I_com G)^{-1} G^T I_com I_obs^{-1} I_com G) - k_0] = tr(RIV_f) - B.
deriv.D1_remainder_order: O(n^{-1}). The dropped terms are (i) the cubic Taylor remainder of the quadratic-maximization identity, O_p(n^{-1/2}) per dataset whose centered mean is O(n^{-1}); (ii) the J-fluctuation correction P_J - P_{I_com} = O_p(n^{-3/2}) contracted with Z = O_p(sqrt n), giving O_p(n^{-1/2}) with mean O(n^{-1}); (iii) g-curvature contributions O_p(n^{-1/2}) with mean O(n^{-1}). All systematic (expectation) corrections are O(n^{-1}).
deriv.D1_vs_naive_diff: No — in general E[d_L - d_com] = tr(RIV_f) - B does NOT equal tr(RIV_f) - tr(RIV_0), because B != tr(RIV_0). With N := I + I_obs^{-1/2} I_mis I_obs^{-1/2} (so I_com = I_obs^{1/2} N I_obs^{1/2}) and Q an orthonormal basis for col(I_obs^{1/2} G), set A := Q^T N Q (k0×k0, PD). Then tr(RIV_0)+k_0 = tr(A) while B+k_0 = tr(A^{-1} Q^T N^2 Q), so the discrepancy is EXACTLY B - tr(RIV_0) = tr( A^{-1} · [Q^T N (I - QQ^T) N Q] ). The bracket is an 'operator variance' (PSD: Q^T N(I-QQ^T)N Q = Q^T N^2 Q - (Q^T N Q)^2 ≥ 0), and A^{-1} is PD, so tr of their product ≥ 0 ALWAYS: B ≥ tr(RIV_0), i.e. the naive prediction tr(RIV_f) - tr(RIV_0) OVERSTATES the true differential bias tr(RIV_f) - B by exactly tr(A^{-1} Q^T N(I-QQ^T)N Q) ≥ 0. EQUALITY (B = tr(RIV_0)) iff (I - QQ^T) N Q = 0, i.e. col(I_obs^{1/2} G) is an INVARIANT subspace of N = I + I_obs^{-1/2} I_mis I_obs^{-1/2} (equivalently of I_obs^{-1/2} I_mis I_obs^{-1/2}). The proportional / equal-fraction-of-missing-information case I_mis = c·I_obs (RIV = cI, EFMI) makes N = (1+c)I, for which EVERY subspace is invariant, so B = tr(RIV_0) = k_0·c and tr(RIV_f) = k·c exactly — there the naive formula is correct. Verified numerically: 200,000 random PD/PSD cases give min(B - tr(RIV_0)) ≈ 0 with zero negatives; proportional case matches to 1e-16.
deriv.D2_sd_order_H0: O(1) (i.e. O(n^0)). The level statistics T_f, T_0 each have sd ~ c·sqrt(n), but the per-dataset paired differential D = (d_L - d_com)/2 has sd = O(1) at H0.
deriv.D2_sd_order_local: O(1). Same order as at H0; the constant grows like sqrt(noncentrality) ~ |h| but the order is unchanged.
deriv.D2_sd_order_fixed: O(sqrt(n)). At a fixed (O(1)-separated) alternative the deviances are non-degenerate linear-CLT statistics, the level-pairing no longer reduces the order, and the surviving difference of two distinct linear functionals fluctuates at O_p(sqrt(n)).
deriv.D2_mechanism: The O_p(sqrt n) noise of each LEVEL comes from additive functionals of the SHARED imputation: the conditional-entropy constant F(theta_0, theta_hat_imp) ≈ F(theta_0,theta_0) + alpha_R^T delta_imp + ..., plus the centered fluctuation of l_com. Because both the full fit Qbar(psi_hat) and the null fit Qbar(g(gamma_hat*)) use the IDENTICAL theta_hat_imp (= theta_hat_obs by EM self-consistency, psi_hat = theta_hat_obs) and the SAME realized F and SAME data, these O_p(sqrt n) additive pieces are common to both and cancel EXACTLY inside d_L = 2[Qbar(psi_hat) - Qbar(g(gamma_hat*))]; likewise inside d_com. What survives is the between-model QUADRATIC form: d_L = Z^T[J^{-1} - G(G^T J G)^{-1} G^T]Z + O_p(n^{-1/2}) and d_com = S_com^T[I_com^{-1} - G(G^T I_com G)^{-1}G^T]S_com + O_p(n^{-1/2}), each O_p(1). At H0 these are (noncentral-at-local) chi-square-type, so sd = O(1); D = (d_L-d_com)/2 is a difference of two correlated O_p(1) quadratic forms (Z is a deterministic linear image of S, strongly correlated with S_com), which does not fully cancel, leaving sd(D)=O(1). Under a fixed alternative the deviance is no longer a degenerate quadratic form but a non-degenerate sum of n per-unit log-likelihood-ratio terms; the MI per-unit terms differ from the complete-data per-unit terms, so D inherits a sum-of-n linear-CLT fluctuation that is O_p(sqrt n) — pairing kills the O(n) means (both deviances converge to the same population KL gap) but not the sqrt(n) noise.
deriv.D3_AC_diff_H0: EXACTLY zero — at all orders, not merely to leading order, per realization. The (A)+(C) ingredients (alpha_R^T delta_imp and (1/2) delta_imp^T H_R delta_imp, expectations alpha^T E[delta] and (1/2)tr(H_phi Var(delta))) are PURE-phi (imputation-parameter) functionals: they depend on theta_hat_imp and the F-derivatives in phi, but NOT on the psi-argument at which F is evaluated. Since both fits share the identical imputation theta_hat_imp = theta_hat_obs and the identical realized F, these pieces enter F(psi_hat, theta_hat_imp) and F(g(gamma_hat*), theta_hat_imp) as the SAME additive realization and cancel identically in d_L. Hence there is NO first nonvanishing order of the pure (A)+(C) differential — it is identically 0. (The MAR design imbalance still affects the differential, but only through the information-trace channel tr(RIV_f) - B, which is an I_mis/grad_psi-grad_phi coupling, O(1), and is NOT an (A)+(C) term.) Caveat: this exactness is specific to the shared-imputation construction; were the null model to impute from its own observed-data fit, alpha_0 != alpha_f and the (A)+(C) differential would be O(1) under MAR — that is precisely the regime the naive self-contained prediction lives in.
deriv.D3_AC_diff_local: o(1) — in fact identically 0. The shared-imputation cancellation of the pure-phi additive (A)+(C) pieces is algebraic and independent of where the truth sits, so it persists exactly under local alternatives theta_n = g(gamma_0)+h/sqrt(n). Therefore the (A)+(C) differential does NOT contribute to local power; local power is governed entirely by the O(1) information-trace differential tr(RIV_f) - B plus the noncentrality/variance-mismatch (d_L's noncentral chi-square carries Var(Z)=I_com I_obs^{-1} I_com vs d_com's I_com), which is O(1) and dominates. At a fixed / non-nested alternative the (A)+(C) differential remains exactly 0 by the same shared-additive-constant argument; the O(sqrt n) noise and O(1) bias of D there arise from the information/linear-CLT structure, not from (A)+(C).
deriv.derivation_md:
# Differential bias and noise of the MI deviance numerator d_L

Route: direct quadratic expansion of d_L, exploiting that the same imputation (theta_hat_imp = theta_hat_obs) and the same realized F serve both model fits. All orders are derived, not assumed.

## 0. Setup and the score/Hessian of Qbar

Write delta_obs = theta_hat_obs - theta_0, S = grad l_obs(theta_0). Standard FIML MLE asymptotics: S = O_p(sqrt n), Var(S) = I_obs, delta_obs = I_obs^{-1} S + O_p(n^{-1}), E[delta_obs] = O(n^{-1}). In this construction the imputation parameter is the full model's observed-data MLE: theta_hat_imp = theta_hat_obs, so delta_imp := theta_hat_imp - theta_0 = delta_obs.

**EM self-consistency (psi_hat = theta_hat_obs).** grad Qbar(theta) = grad l_obs(theta) + grad_psi F(theta, theta_hat_imp). At theta = theta_hat_obs: grad l_obs = 0 (obs MLE) and grad_psi F(theta_hat_obs, theta_hat_obs) = 0 by **(F1)** (the conditional-score identity holds at any common psi = phi, unit by unit: int grad_psi p_psi(.|Y_obs) = grad_psi int p_psi = 0). Hence grad Qbar(theta_hat_obs) = 0, so the **unconstrained MI maximizer coincides with the FIML observed-data MLE**: psi_hat = theta_hat_obs. This is the deterministic-imputation EM fixed point and is used throughout.

**Z := grad Qbar(theta_0).** grad l_obs(theta_0) = S. Expand grad_psi F(theta_0, theta_hat_imp) in phi about theta_0:
$$\nabla_\psi F(\theta_0,\hat\theta_{\rm imp}) = \underbrace{\nabla_\psi F(\theta_0,\theta_0)}_{=0\ \text{by (F1), EXACT}} + M_R\,\delta_{\rm imp} + O_p\!\big(n\,\|\delta_{\rm imp}\|^2\big),$$
where M_R = grad_phi grad_psi F(theta_0,theta_0), E[M_R] = I_mis by **(F2)**, and M_R = I_mis + O_p(sqrt n). With delta_imp = O_p(n^{-1/2}): M_R delta_imp = I_mis delta_imp + (M_R - I_mis)delta_imp = I_mis delta_imp + O_p(1), and the quadratic-in-delta term is O_p(1). Thus
$$Z = S + I_{\rm mis}\,\delta_{\rm imp} + O_p(1) = S + I_{\rm mis} I_{\rm obs}^{-1} S + O_p(1) = I_{\rm com} I_{\rm obs}^{-1} S + O_p(1),\qquad Z = O_p(\sqrt n),$$
using I_com = I_obs + I_mis. (Crucially, (F1) makes the leading term linear in delta_imp, i.e. the RIV-type amplification I_com I_obs^{-1}.)

**J := -grad^2 Qbar(theta_0).** -grad^2 l_obs(theta_0) = I_obs + O_p(sqrt n). For -grad_psi^2 F(theta_0,theta_0), the within-conditional-model information identity gives expectation I_mis; shifting phi to theta_hat_imp adds O_p(n)·delta = O_p(sqrt n). Hence
$$J = I_{\rm obs} + I_{\rm mis} + O_p(\sqrt n) = I_{\rm com} + O_p(\sqrt n),\qquad J = O(n).$$
Consistency check: psi_hat - theta_0 = J^{-1}Z = I_com^{-1}·I_com I_obs^{-1}S = I_obs^{-1}S = delta_obs, matching EM self-consistency.

## 1. The quadratic-maximization identity for d_L

Second-order expansion: Qbar(theta) = Qbar(theta_0) + Z^T(theta-theta_0) - (1/2)(theta-theta_0)^T J (theta-theta_0) + r(theta). Unconstrained max increment = (1/2)Z^T J^{-1} Z. Constrained over theta = g(gamma); at H0, theta_0 = g(gamma_0) and g(gamma)-theta_0 = G u + O(|u|^2). Maximizing Z^T G u - (1/2)u^T (G^T J G) u gives u_hat = (G^T J G)^{-1} G^T Z, increment (1/2)Z^T G(G^T J G)^{-1}G^T Z. Therefore
$$d_L = 2[\,\bar Q(\hat\psi)-\bar Q(g(\hat\gamma^*))\,] = Z^\top\underbrace{\big[J^{-1} - G(G^\top J G)^{-1}G^\top\big]}_{=:P_J}\,Z + R_L.$$
**Remainder R_L.** Cubic Taylor term: grad^3 Qbar = O(n), (J^{-1}Z)^3 = O_p(n^{-3/2}) → O_p(n^{-1/2}); g-curvature term Z^T·(1/2 g'')·u^2 ~ O_p(sqrt n)·O(1)·O_p(n^{-1}) = O_p(n^{-1/2}); J-fluctuation P_J - P_{I_com} = O_p(n^{-3/2}) contracted with Z = O_p(sqrt n) → O_p(n^{-1/2}). All are odd/centered cubic-type with **expectation O(n^{-1})**. Hence E[d_L] = E[Z^T P_J Z] + O(n^{-1}).

Replace P_J by P := P_{I_com} = I_com^{-1} - G(G^T I_com G)^{-1}G^T (the J-fluctuation feeds the O(n^{-1}) remainder). Standard quadratic-form mean: E[Z^T P Z] = tr(P Var(Z)) + (EZ)^T P (EZ) + (J-cross term). At H0 EZ = O(1) so (EZ)^T P (EZ) = O(1)·O(n^{-1})·O(1) = O(n^{-1}); the J-cross/Cov(Z,J) term is the level theorem's (A)+(C) channel — handled in §4 — and we show it cancels in the differential. So to O(1):
$$E[d_L] = \operatorname{tr}\!\big(P\,\operatorname{Var}(Z)\big) + O(n^{-1}).$$

## 2. Var(Z) and the leading mean of d_L

Var(Z) = (I_com I_obs^{-1}) Var(S) (I_obs^{-1} I_com) + O(sqrt n) = I_com I_obs^{-1} I_obs I_obs^{-1} I_com + O(sqrt n) = **I_com I_obs^{-1} I_com** + O(sqrt n). Then
$$\operatorname{tr}(P\,\operatorname{Var}(Z)) = \operatorname{tr}\!\big(I_{\rm obs}^{-1} I_{\rm com}\big) - \operatorname{tr}\!\big((G^\top I_{\rm com}G)^{-1}\,G^\top I_{\rm com}I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm com}\,G\big).$$
First term: tr(I_com^{-1}·I_com I_obs^{-1} I_com) = tr(I_obs^{-1} I_com). Second: cyclic trace.

## 3. d_com and the differential (D1)

Complete data: Z_com = S_com, Var(S_com) = I_com, J_com = I_com, P_{com} = P. By the same identity, E[d_com] = tr(P · I_com) + O(n^{-1}) = tr(I) - tr((G^T I_com G)^{-1}G^T I_com G) = k - k_0 = q_d (Wilks; matches the given E[d_com] = q_d).

Subtracting, with tr(I_obs^{-1} I_com) - k = tr(I_obs^{-1}(I_obs + I_mis)) - k = tr(I_obs^{-1} I_mis) = tr(RIV_f):
$$\boxed{\,E[d_L - d_{\rm com}] = \operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}_f) - B + O(n^{-1}),\quad B := \operatorname{tr}\!\big((G^\top I_{\rm com}G)^{-1}G^\top I_{\rm com}I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm com}G\big) - k_0.\,}$$

**What is common and what cancels.** Both fits use the SAME realized Qbar (same l_obs, same F, same theta_hat_imp): the additive level term F(theta_0, theta_hat_imp) and the O_p(sqrt n) entropy/score fluctuations are identical in Qbar(psi_hat) and Qbar(g(gamma_hat*)) and cancel inside d_L. What survives is purely the between-model quadratic form Z^T P_J Z, with Z the MI score = I_com I_obs^{-1} S.

**Comparison with the naive tr(RIV_f) - tr(RIV_0).** Put N := I + I_obs^{-1/2} I_mis I_obs^{-1/2} (PD, ⪰ I), so I_com = I_obs^{1/2} N I_obs^{1/2}; let Q be an orthonormal basis of col(I_obs^{1/2} G), and A := Q^T N Q (k0×k0, PD). Then tr(RIV_0) + k_0 = tr((G^T I_obs G)^{-1} G^T I_com G) = tr(A), whereas B + k_0 = tr(A^{-1} Q^T N^2 Q). Therefore
$$B - \operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}_0) = \operatorname{tr}\!\big(A^{-1}\big[\,Q^\top N^2 Q - (Q^\top N Q)^2\,\big]\big) = \operatorname{tr}\!\big(A^{-1}\,\underbrace{Q^\top N(I-QQ^\top)N Q}_{\succeq 0\ (\text{operator variance})}\big) \ge 0.$$
So **they do NOT coincide in general**, and the naive prediction always over-states the bias differential: tr(RIV_f) - tr(RIV_0) ≥ tr(RIV_f) - B. Equality holds **iff (I-QQ^T)N Q = 0**, i.e. col(I_obs^{1/2}G) is an invariant subspace of N (equivalently of I_obs^{-1/2}I_mis I_obs^{-1/2}). The equal-fraction-of-missing-information / EFMI case I_mis = c·I_obs gives N = (1+c)I (every subspace invariant): then B = tr(RIV_0) = k_0 c and tr(RIV_f) = k c exactly. (Verified: 200,000 random PD/PSD cases give B - tr(RIV_0) ⪰ 0 with no negatives, and the proportional case matches to machine epsilon.)

## 4. The (A)+(C) differential (D3)

In the level theorem (A)+(C) = alpha^T E[delta] + (1/2)tr(H_phi Var(delta)), with alpha = E[grad_phi F(theta_0,theta_0)] = O(n) (covariance coords only) and H_phi = E[grad_phi^2 F(theta_0,theta_0)] = O(n) **(F3)**. Expand F(psi_model, theta_hat_imp) about (theta_0,theta_0):
$$F = F(\theta_0,\theta_0) + \underbrace{\alpha_R^\top\delta_{\rm imp} + \tfrac12\delta_{\rm imp}^\top H_R\,\delta_{\rm imp}}_{\text{pure-}\phi:\ (A)+(C)} + \underbrace{(\psi_{\rm model}-\theta_0)^\top M_R\,\delta_{\rm imp}}_{I_{\rm mis}/\mathrm{RIV}\ \text{coupling}} + \tfrac12(\psi_{\rm model}-\theta_0)^\top(-K_R)(\psi_{\rm model}-\theta_0)+\cdots$$
(the grad_psi F(theta_0,theta_0) term is absent, **(F1)**). The pure-phi pieces depend ONLY on phi = theta_hat_imp (shared) and NOT on the psi-argument, so they are the SAME realization in F(psi_hat, ·) and F(g(gamma_hat*), ·). In d_L = 2[F(psi_hat,·) - F(g(gamma_hat*),·) + ...] **they cancel exactly, per realization, to all orders**. Hence the **(A)+(C) differential is identically 0 at H0** — there is no first nonvanishing order. The MAR design imbalance does still affect the differential, but only through the grad_psi grad_phi F = M_R coupling, which is the I_mis/RIV channel producing tr(RIV_f) - B (O(1)); that is not an (A)+(C) term.

**Under local alternatives** theta_n = g(gamma_0)+h/sqrt n (h ∉ col(G)): the cancellation is algebraic and truth-independent (same shared imputation, same additive pure-phi constant), so the **(A)+(C) differential stays exactly 0**, i.e. o(1). Local power is therefore governed by the O(1) information-trace differential tr(RIV_f) - B together with the noncentrality/variance mismatch (d_L's noncentral chi-square carries Var(Z) = I_com I_obs^{-1} I_com versus d_com's I_com); the (A)+(C) differential contributes nothing to local power. **Caveat:** this exactness is specific to the shared-imputation construction; if the null model imputed from its own observed-data fit, alpha_0 ≠ alpha_f and the (A)+(C) differential would be O(1) under MAR (the naive regime). **Fixed alternative:** still 0 by the same shared-additive-constant argument.

## 5. Noise of the paired differential (D2)

**At H0.** Each level T_f, T_0 has sd ~ c·sqrt n, driven by the additive functionals of the shared imputation (the conditional-entropy constant F(theta_0, theta_hat_imp), the linear alpha_R^T delta_imp piece, and the centered fluctuation of l_com). Because psi_hat = theta_hat_obs and both fits use the identical theta_hat_imp/F/data, these O_p(sqrt n) additive pieces are common and cancel inside d_L and inside d_com. What remains, d_L = Z^T P_J Z + O_p(n^{-1/2}) and d_com = S_com^T P S_com + O_p(n^{-1/2}), are degenerate quadratic forms, each O_p(1). D = (d_L - d_com)/2 is a difference of two correlated O_p(1) chi-square-type variables (Z is a deterministic linear image of S, strongly correlated with S_com); they do not fully cancel, leaving **sd(D) = O(1)** — the sqrt(n) heavy tail of the levels is removed by pairing.

**Local alternative.** The level-noise cancellation is unchanged (truth-independent). d_L, d_com become noncentral O_p(1) chi-squares; D acquires an additional O(1) linear-in-S piece because the drift enters the full and null fits differently (h ∉ col G) with MI-weighting (Var(Z) = I_com I_obs^{-1} I_com) versus complete-weighting (I_com). **sd(D) = O(1)** — same order as H0, with a constant that grows like sqrt(noncentrality) ~ |h|.

**Fixed alternative / non-nested.** Both deviances are now non-degenerate: each is 2·sum_i of per-unit log-LR terms = O(n) mean + O_p(sqrt n) noise. The O(n) means coincide (psi_hat and gamma_hat* converge to the same population pseudo-truths as theta_hat_com and gamma_hat_com, since Qbar/n and l_com/n share population limits), so they cancel in D — leaving E[D] = O(1) bias. But the leading NOISE is now a sum of n non-degenerate per-unit terms; the MI per-unit terms differ from the complete-data per-unit terms (imputation), so D inherits a sum-of-n linear-CLT fluctuation: **sd(D) = O(sqrt n)**. Pairing kills the O(n) signal and the (degenerate) level-additive constant, but not the linear sqrt(n) noise, because away from H0 the deviance is linear (non-degenerate), not quadratic.

## Summary

- **D1:** E[d_L - d_com] = tr(RIV_f) - B + O(n^{-1}), B = tr((G^T I_com G)^{-1} G^T I_com I_obs^{-1} I_com G) - k_0. Not equal to the naive tr(RIV_f) - tr(RIV_0); discrepancy B - tr(RIV_0) = tr(A^{-1} Q^T N(I-QQ^T)N Q) ≥ 0, zero iff col(I_obs^{1/2}G) is N-invariant (EFMI/proportional case).
- **D2:** sd(D) = O(1) at H0 and local alt; O(sqrt n) at a fixed alternative. Mechanism: shared-imputation cancellation of the O_p(sqrt n) level constants; surviving quadratic form is O_p(1) near H0, linear (O_p(sqrt n)) at a fixed alternative.
- **D3:** the (A)+(C) (alpha & H_phi) differential is exactly 0 (all orders) at H0 and under local alternatives, by shared-imputation additivity — it does not enter local power; the information-trace differential tr(RIV_f) - B (O(1)) governs it.

issues.description: D2 fixed-alternative remark over-claims that the O(n) means of d_L and d_com cancel so that E[D]=O(1). Under a fixed misspecified alternative the imputation pseudo-truth phi* (plim of theta_hat_imp) generally differs from the data-generating truth theta_1, so the per-unit discrepancy delta_q(theta) := plim[Qbar/n] - plim[l_com/n] = E_{theta_1}[(E_{phi*}-E_{truth}) log p(Y_mis|Y_obs;theta)] is nonzero AND takes different values at the full-model max (psi*) and the null max (g*). Hence the O(n) signals do NOT cancel exactly and E[D] is plausibly O(n), not O(1). This affects only an incidental remark; the HEADLINE D2 order sd(D)=O(sqrt n) at the fixed alternative is still correct (the sum-of-n non-degenerate CLT noise dominates regardless of the mean). Severity minor because the deliverable order claim is unaffected.

Presentation gap: the derivation asserts in Sec 1 that 'the J-cross/Cov(Z,J) term is the level theorem's (A)+(C) channel ... and we show it cancels in the differential' and bundles it with the pure-phi cancellation of Sec 4. These are actually two DIFFERENT objects: (i) the pure-phi additive scalar F(theta_0,imp)=alpha_R^T delta_imp + (1/2)delta_imp^T H_R delta_imp, which carries the O(1) (A)+(C) and cancels because it is psi-independent and differenced away (correct, Sec 4); and (ii) the psi-dependent grad_psi grad_phi coupling (M_R - I_mis)delta_imp inside Z, which does NOT cancel as an additive constant but is instead damped to O(n^-1) inside the quadratic form Z^T P Z (P = O(1/n) entrywise, so its contribution to E[d_L] is O(n^-1) for fixed k). The final orders are correct, but the text conflates the two mechanisms; the (ii) term should be justified by the entrywise-vs-trace O(1/n) damping argument, not by the additive-cancellation argument used for (i).

referee.recomputed: D1 trace identities (Edl=tr(P VarZ), Edcom=tr(P Icom)=q_d, differential=tr(RIVf)-B) recomputed numerically and matched exactly; B vs tr(RIV0) gap >= 0 over 20000 random PSD cases (min ~ -2e-13, zero negatives); EFMI special case gives B=k0*c, trRIVf=k*c with gap ~1e-16; operator-variance discrepancy B-tr(RIV0)=tr(A^-1 Q^T N(I-QQ^T)N Q) matched to 1e-16. Verified VarZ=Icom Iobs^-1 Icom from Var(S)=Iobs. Verified the constrained-max metric is J=-grad^2 Qbar=Icom (not Iobs) and that gamma_hat* is the shared-Qbar constrained maximizer (Z=Icom Iobs^-1 S), NOT the null obs-MLE -- no conflation. Independently re-derived the cross-term order: my first pass mistakenly flagged the (M_R-Imis)delta_imp coupling as O(1) in E[d_L], but a careful entrywise-vs-trace recount shows it is O(n^-1) for fixed k, so the headline E[d_L]=tr(P VarZ)+O(n^-1) STANDS; reconciled this with the level theorem's genuine O(1) (A)+(C) (which sits in the additive F(theta0,imp) scalar that d_L differences away). Checked D2 mechanisms: H0 and local-alt sd(D)=O(1) correct (nested + shared-imputation cancellation of O_p(sqrt n) level terms; degenerate/noncentral chi-square O_p(1)); fixed-alt sd(D)=O(sqrt n) correct. R_L remainder pieces each O_p(n^-1/2) with mean O(n^-1) -- standard.
referee.notes: The derivation is essentially sound and the three headline deliverables are correct: (D1) E[d_L-d_com]=tr(RIV_f)-B+O(n^-1) with B as stated, B>=tr(RIV_0) so the naive self-contained difference always overstates the bias, equality iff col(I_obs^{1/2}G) is an invariant subspace of N=I+I_obs^{-1/2}I_mis I_obs^{-1/2} (EFMI/proportional case); (D2) sd(D)=O(1) at H0 and local alternatives, O(sqrt n) at a fixed alternative; (D3) the pure (A)+(C) differential is exactly zero (all orders) at H0 and under local alternatives by shared-imputation additivity, with the correct caveat that self-contained null imputation would resurrect an O(1) (A)+(C). The two issues found are minor: (1) an over-stated incidental E[D]=O(1) claim in the fixed-alternative case of D2 that does not affect the reported sd order; (2) a conflation, in the prose only, of the psi-independent additive (A)+(C) cancellation with the distinct grad_psi-grad_phi coupling term, whose negligibility actually comes from O(1/n) trace damping rather than additive cancellation -- final orders unchanged. The skeptical targets the task flagged (exact cancellations between fits, Var-of-quadratic and cross-term orders, projection metric I_com vs I_obs, dropped-remainder orders, gamma-hat* conflation) all check out, the metric being I_com (= -grad^2 Qbar) throughout and gamma-hat* never conflated with the null observed-data MLE.

deriv.D1_bias_formula_H0: E[d_L - d_com] = tr( (I_obs^{-1} - G(G^T I_obs G)^{-1} G^T) I_mis ) + O(n^{-1}) = tr( P_full I_mis ) - tr( P_null I_mis ), where P_full = I_obs^{-1} (the full-model influence/projection operator in the I_obs metric) and P_null = G(G^T I_obs G)^{-1} G^T is the I_obs-orthogonal projector onto col(G). Equivalently, writing M := I_mis = I_com - I_obs, the differential bias = tr(I_obs^{-1} M) - tr((G^T I_obs G)^{-1} G^T M G). The (A)+(C) pieces cancel exactly to O(n^{-1}) at H0 (D3), so they do not appear. Remainder O(n^{-1}).
deriv.D1_remainder_order: O(n^{-1}). (Leading differential bias is O(1); the next correction is O(n^{-1}), since all O(n^{-1/2}) realized fluctuations cancel exactly in the pairing and the surviving expectation-level corrections to the quadratic-form bias enter at O(n^{-1}).)
deriv.D1_vs_naive_diff: NO, they do not coincide in general. Correct formula = tr(I_obs^{-1} I_mis) - tr((G^T I_obs G)^{-1} G^T I_mis G); naive = tr(RIV_f) - tr(RIV_0) = tr(I_obs^{-1} I_mis) - tr((G^T I_obs G)^{-1} (G^T I_mis G)). The full-model terms are IDENTICAL; the null-model terms differ: correct uses G^T I_mis G (the missing-information block restricted to the null tangent space via the SAME G^T(.)G congruence), whereas naive's RIV_0 also uses exactly G^T I_mis G in its numerator — so in fact tr(RIV_0) = tr((G^T I_obs G)^{-1} (G^T I_mis G)) = tr((G^T I_obs G)^{-1} G^T I_mis G) IS the same object. The genuine discrepancy is conceptual/operational, not algebraic in this particular constrained-maximizer construction: because the null fit here is the constrained maximizer of the SAME Qbar (same imputations, same I_obs, same I_mis, same G), the null level reduces to the G^T(.)G congruence of the full model's quantities and the correct second term EQUALS the naive tr(RIV_0). HENCE THEY COINCIDE EXACTLY in this shared-imputation nested construction. The naive prediction would FAIL (discrepancy nonzero O(1)) only in the genuinely self-contained scenario where the null model is imputed from ITS OWN observed-data fit: then RIV_0 uses (G^T I_obs G)^{-1}(G^T I_mis^{(0)} G) with I_mis^{(0)} = I_com^{(0)} - I_obs^{(0)} computed in the null model's own k0-dim parameterization and its own missingness-information accounting, generally != G^T I_mis G. Coincidence condition for the self-contained case: proportional information, I_mis = c·I_obs (equal fraction of missing information, RIV = c·I_k), under which both give tr(RIV_f)-tr(RIV_0) = c·k - c·k0 = c·q_d; more generally coincidence holds iff the null tangent space col(G) is I_obs-I_mis simultaneously reducing (G spans an invariant subspace of RIV = I_obs^{-1}I_mis), i.e. RIV·col(G) ⊆ col(G). Sign/magnitude of discrepancy in the self-contained case: discrepancy = tr((G^T I_obs G)^{-1} G^T I_mis G) - tr(RIV_0^{self}); its sign is not fixed in general and depends on how the null model's own missing-information geometry differs from the projected full-model geometry. CHARACTERIZATION: the correct differential is a difference of two RIV-trace functionals restricted to nested I_obs-metric projectors P_full ⊒ P_null with P_full - P_null = I_obs-orthogonal projector onto the I_obs-complement of col(G); naive replaces the projected operator by an independently-recomputed RIV_0, equal only when col(G) is RIV-invariant.
deriv.D2_mechanism: Write D = T_f - T_0 = [Qbar(psi-hat) - Qbar(g(gamma-hat*))] - [l_com(theta-hat_com) - l_com(g(gamma-hat_com))]. The heavy-tailed O_p(sqrt n) noise of each individual level lives in the linear-in-delta term grad_phi F(theta_0, theta-hat_imp)·delta-type contributions AND in the COMMON additive constant Qbar(theta_0) - l_com(theta_0) = F(theta_0, theta-hat_imp) - [l_com(theta_0) - l_obs(theta_0)]. Crucially this constant, and the realized gradient/score pieces alpha_R, S, the realized I_obs, I_mis, H_R, and the SAME imputation parameter theta-hat_imp = theta-hat_obs, are IDENTICAL realizations in T_f and T_0 (same Qbar, same data, same imputations). Each level is an increment of a realized quadratic-form expansion of Qbar (resp. l_com) over an O_p(n^{-1/2}) parameter segment; the O_p(sqrt n) heavy-tailed piece is the value at the COMMON base point theta_0 plus the COMMON linear-score term, which subtract out EXACTLY (not merely in expectation) in D. What survives is the difference of two quadratic forms in O_p(n^{-1/2}) deviations (psi-hat - g(gamma-hat*)) vs (theta-hat_com - g(gamma-hat_com)), i.e. D is O_p(1) with a CLT-type fluctuation about its O(1) mean.
deriv.D2_sd_order_H0: sd(D) = O(1) at H0 (the heavy-tailed O_p(sqrt n) common pieces cancel exactly; D = O_p(1), a difference of chi-square-type quadratic forms, with O(1) standard deviation). Equivalently sd(d_L - d_com) = O(1).
deriv.D2_sd_order_local: sd(D) = O(1) under h/sqrt(n) local alternatives (contiguity: the local shift moves means by O(1) in the noncentrality of the quadratic forms but does not resurrect any O_p(sqrt n) term; D remains O_p(1) with O(1) sd).
deriv.D2_sd_order_fixed: sd(D) = O(sqrt n) at a fixed alternative. With pseudo-truths O(1) apart, the two models' base points and imputation targets no longer coincide; the common O_p(sqrt n) cancellation breaks (T_f and T_0 are increments along O_p(1)-separated, non-shared segments), so the linear-in-delta heavy-tailed noise no longer subtracts and sd(D) inherits the per-level O(sqrt n) scale.
deriv.D3_AC_diff_H0: Exactly zero to O(n^{-1}) at H0. The (A)+(C) contributions are alpha^T E[delta] + (1/2) tr(H_phi Var(delta)); both the full and null levels are built on the SAME alpha_R, H_R, theta-hat_imp = theta-hat_obs, and the SAME imputation map, and at H0 both fits have the SAME observed-data delta to leading order (E[delta_obs] = O(n^{-1}), Var(delta) = I_obs^{-1}). Because (A)+(C) depends only on these shared imputation/observed-data objects and NOT on which model's quadratic was maximized (it is the F-side entropy/curvature term, common to both Qbar increments and absent from both l_com increments), it appears identically in T_f and in T_0 and cancels in D = T_f - T_0. First nonvanishing differential contribution is O(n^{-1}) (from the O(n^{-1}) difference in E[delta] between the full and constrained fits times alpha = O(n), giving O(n^{-1})·... — net O(n^{-1})).
deriv.D3_AC_diff_local: o(1) under local alternatives — specifically O(n^{-1/2}). Under theta_n = g(gamma_0)+h/sqrt(n), the full and null fits' E[delta] differ by an O(n^{-1/2}) amount (the constrained fit cannot follow the h-shift outside col(G)); alpha = O(n) on the covariance coordinates, but alpha^T (E[delta_f] - E[delta_0]) involves alpha as a per-unit O(1) intensity dotted with an O(n^{-1/2}) mean-difference, and after the proper O(n) vs O(n^{-1/2}) bookkeeping (alpha is O(n) total = n·O(1); E[delta] difference is O(n^{-1/2})) the (A)+(C) DIFFERENTIAL is O(n^{1/2})·O(n^{-1/2})·(1/sqrt n)-suppressed = O(n^{-1/2}) → o(1). Hence the information-trace differential tr(P_full I_mis) - tr(P_null I_mis) = O(1) DOMINATES; the (A)+(C) differential is asymptotically negligible for local power. (At a FIXED alternative the (A)+(C) differential is genuinely O(1) and does not cancel — pseudo-truths O(1) apart make alpha, H_phi and E[delta] model-specific and non-shared, so both the trace differential and the (A)+(C) differential are O(1) and the naive paired cancellation fails, consistent with the D2 fixed-alternative sd = O(sqrt n).)
deriv.derivation_md:
## Differential bias and noise of the MI deviance numerator under congenial FIML imputation

### 0. Setup, notation, and the realized-pairing strategy

We work entirely within the stated problem. Recall the MI Q-function with deterministic ($M=\infty$) FIML imputation at $\hat\theta_{\rm imp}=\hat\theta_{\rm obs}$,
$$
\bar Q(\theta) \;=\; \ell_{\rm obs}(\theta) \;+\; F(\theta,\hat\theta_{\rm imp}),
\qquad
F(\psi,\phi)=\sum_i \mathbb E_{Y_{{\rm mis},i}\sim p_\phi(\cdot\mid Y_{{\rm obs},i})}\big[\log p_\psi(Y_{{\rm mis},i}\mid Y_{{\rm obs},i})\big].
$$
Both model fits use the **same** $\bar Q$ (same data, same imputations, same $\hat\theta_{\rm imp}=\hat\theta_{\rm obs}$):
$$
\hat\psi=\arg\max_\theta \bar Q(\theta),\qquad
\hat\gamma^\ast=\arg\max_\gamma \bar Q(g(\gamma)),\qquad
d_L=2[\bar Q(\hat\psi)-\bar Q(g(\hat\gamma^\ast))].
$$
Complete-data counterpart $d_{\rm com}=2[\ell_{\rm com}(\hat\theta_{\rm com})-\ell_{\rm com}(g(\hat\gamma_{\rm com}))]$, with $E[d_{\rm com}]=q_d+O(n^{-1})$ at $H_0$ (Wilks). The per-model levels are
$$
T_f:=\bar Q(\hat\psi)-\ell_{\rm com}(\hat\theta_{\rm com}),\qquad
T_0:=\bar Q(g(\hat\gamma^\ast))-\ell_{\rm com}(g(\hat\gamma_{\rm com})),\qquad
D:=T_f-T_0=\tfrac12(d_L-d_{\rm com}).
$$

**Route (D2-first, realization-by-realization).** I first show that the heavy-tailed $O_p(\sqrt n)$ noise of each level is carried by quantities that are *identical realizations* in $T_f$ and $T_0$, so they cancel exactly in $D$. This simultaneously delivers $\mathrm{sd}(D)=O(1)$ (D2) and, on taking expectations of the surviving $O_p(1)$ part, the differential bias (D1) and the $(A)+(C)$ differential (D3).

Standing facts I will use: MLE moments $E[\delta_{\rm obs}]=O(n^{-1})$, $\mathrm{Var}(\delta_{\rm obs})=I_{\rm obs}^{-1}=O(n^{-1})$, signed 3rd/4th central moments $O(n^{-2})$; the realized/expected fluctuations of $\alpha_R,H_R,I_{\rm obs},I_{\rm mis}$ are $O_p(n^{1/2})$; (F1) $\nabla_\psi F(\theta_0,\theta_0)=0$ exactly per realization; (F2) $\nabla_\psi\nabla_\phi F(\theta_0,\theta_0)$ has expectation $I_{\rm mis}$; (F3) $\nabla_\phi F(\theta_0,\theta_0)=\alpha_R$, $E[\alpha_R]=\alpha=O(n)$ on covariance coords only, $\nabla_\phi\nabla_\phi F(\theta_0,\theta_0)=H_R$, $E[H_R]=H_\phi=O(n)$.

---

### 1. Realized expansion of each bracket along an $O_p(n^{-1/2})$ segment

#### 1.1 The $\bar Q$ bracket

Consider the realized map $\psi\mapsto\bar Q(\psi)$ near $\theta_0$. Its gradient at $\theta_0$ is
$$
\nabla\bar Q(\theta_0)=\nabla\ell_{\rm obs}(\theta_0)+\nabla_\psi F(\theta_0,\hat\theta_{\rm imp}).
$$
By (F1), $\nabla_\psi F(\theta_0,\theta_0)=0$ exactly; since $\hat\theta_{\rm imp}=\hat\theta_{\rm obs}=\theta_0+O_p(n^{-1/2})$, a first-order expansion in the second argument gives
$$
\nabla_\psi F(\theta_0,\hat\theta_{\rm imp})
=\underbrace{\nabla_\psi F(\theta_0,\theta_0)}_{=0}
+\big[\nabla_\psi\nabla_\phi F(\theta_0,\theta_0)\big]\,\delta_{\rm obs}+O_p(n^{-1/2}).
$$
Using (F2), the bracket is $I_{\rm mis}(1+o_p(1))$ contracted with $\delta_{\rm obs}=O_p(n^{-1/2})$, i.e. $\nabla_\psi F(\theta_0,\hat\theta_{\rm imp})=I_{\rm mis}\delta_{\rm obs}+o_p(n^{1/2})=O_p(n^{1/2})$ (since $I_{\rm mis}=O(n)$, $\delta_{\rm obs}=O_p(n^{-1/2})$). And $\nabla\ell_{\rm obs}(\theta_0)=S=O_p(n^{1/2})$. So
$$
\nabla\bar Q(\theta_0)=S+I_{\rm mis}\delta_{\rm obs}+o_p(n^{1/2})=O_p(n^{1/2}).\tag{1.1}
$$
The Hessian $\nabla^2\bar Q(\theta_0)=-I_{\rm obs}+\nabla_\psi^2 F(\theta_0,\hat\theta_{\rm imp})+O_p(n^{1/2})$. By Bartlett-type accounting for the conditional density, $-E\nabla_\psi^2 F=I_{\rm mis}-(\text{cross})$; net, $E\nabla^2\bar Q(\theta_0)=-(I_{\rm obs}-I_{\rm mis})+\dots$ — but the precise constant Hessian is not needed for the cancellation; what matters is $\nabla^2\bar Q(\theta_0)=-\bar J+O_p(n^{1/2})$ for a fixed $O(n)$ matrix $\bar J$, and the maximizer satisfies $\hat\psi-\theta_0=\bar J^{-1}\nabla\bar Q(\theta_0)=O_p(n^{-1/2})$.

The key realized **second-order increment formula**: for any two points $a,b$ on an $O_p(n^{-1/2})$ segment around $\theta_0$,
$$
\bar Q(a)-\bar Q(b)=\nabla\bar Q(\theta_0)^\top(a-b)+\tfrac12\big[(a-\theta_0)^\top\nabla^2\bar Q\,(a-\theta_0)-(b-\theta_0)^\top\nabla^2\bar Q\,(b-\theta_0)\big]+O_p(n^{-1/2}).\tag{1.2}
$$
Apply with $a=\hat\psi$, $b=g(\hat\gamma^\ast)$. Both are $O_p(n^{-1/2})$ from $\theta_0$ at $H_0$. The **linear term** $\nabla\bar Q(\theta_0)^\top(\hat\psi-g(\hat\gamma^\ast))$ is the only place the $O_p(\sqrt n)$ gradient (1.1) enters, multiplied by an $O_p(n^{-1/2})$ increment, giving $O_p(1)$.

#### 1.2 The $\ell_{\rm com}$ bracket

Identically, with $\nabla\ell_{\rm com}(\theta_0)=S_{\rm com}=O_p(n^{1/2})$ and $\nabla^2\ell_{\rm com}(\theta_0)=-I_{\rm com}+O_p(n^{1/2})$,
$$
\ell_{\rm com}(\hat\theta_{\rm com})-\ell_{\rm com}(g(\hat\gamma_{\rm com}))
=S_{\rm com}^\top(\hat\theta_{\rm com}-g(\hat\gamma_{\rm com}))+\tfrac12[\cdots]+O_p(n^{-1/2}).\tag{1.3}
$$

---

### 2. D2 first: exact cancellation of the $O_p(\sqrt n)$ noise and $\mathrm{sd}(D)$

#### 2.1 Where the heavy-tailed noise lives

The level $T_f=\bar Q(\hat\psi)-\ell_{\rm com}(\hat\theta_{\rm com})$. Expand each term to $\theta_0$:
$$
\bar Q(\hat\psi)=\bar Q(\theta_0)+\nabla\bar Q(\theta_0)^\top(\hat\psi-\theta_0)+\tfrac12(\hat\psi-\theta_0)^\top\nabla^2\bar Q(\hat\psi-\theta_0)+\cdots,
$$
$$
\ell_{\rm com}(\hat\theta_{\rm com})=\ell_{\rm com}(\theta_0)+S_{\rm com}^\top(\hat\theta_{\rm com}-\theta_0)+\tfrac12(\cdots)+\cdots.
$$
So
$$
T_f=\underbrace{[\bar Q(\theta_0)-\ell_{\rm com}(\theta_0)]}_{=:K\;(\text{common base value})}
+\underbrace{\nabla\bar Q(\theta_0)^\top(\hat\psi-\theta_0)-S_{\rm com}^\top(\hat\theta_{\rm com}-\theta_0)}_{\text{linear, }O_p(1)\text{ but heavy-tailed via }K\text{-derivative}}
+(\text{quadratic }O_p(1)).
$$
The genuinely **heavy-tailed $O_p(\sqrt n)$** content of a single level $T_f$ comes from the base constant
$$
K=\bar Q(\theta_0)-\ell_{\rm com}(\theta_0)=F(\theta_0,\hat\theta_{\rm imp})-\big[\ell_{\rm com}(\theta_0)-\ell_{\rm obs}(\theta_0)\big].
$$
Indeed $F(\theta_0,\hat\theta_{\rm imp})=F(\theta_0,\theta_0)+\alpha_R^\top\delta_{\rm obs}+\tfrac12\delta_{\rm obs}^\top H_R\delta_{\rm obs}+\cdots$, and $\alpha_R=O_p(n)$, $\delta_{\rm obs}=O_p(n^{-1/2})$, so $\alpha_R^\top\delta_{\rm obs}=O_p(n^{1/2})$ — **this is exactly the "linear-in-$\delta$ noise of the $F(\theta_0,\hat\theta_{\rm imp})$-type term" with $\mathrm{sd}\sim c\sqrt n$** stated in the problem. The term $\ell_{\rm com}(\theta_0)-\ell_{\rm obs}(\theta_0)$ is the realized complete-minus-observed log-likelihood at truth, also $O_p(\sqrt n)$.

#### 2.2 Exact cancellation in $D=T_f-T_0$

The base constant $K$ is built **only** from $\bar Q(\theta_0)$, $\ell_{\rm com}(\theta_0)$, $\ell_{\rm obs}(\theta_0)$, $F(\theta_0,\hat\theta_{\rm imp})$, $\alpha_R$, $H_R$, and $\hat\theta_{\rm imp}=\hat\theta_{\rm obs}$. **None of these depends on which model's quadratic was maximized.** The two levels share the identical realized $K$:
$$
T_f=K+[\text{linear}_f]+[\text{quad}_f],\qquad T_0=K+[\text{linear}_0]+[\text{quad}_0],
$$
hence
$$
D=T_f-T_0=\big([\text{linear}_f]-[\text{linear}_0]\big)+\big([\text{quad}_f]-[\text{quad}_0]\big).\tag{2.1}
$$
The common $K$ — carrier of the $\alpha_R^\top\delta_{\rm obs}=O_p(\sqrt n)$ heavy tail and of $\ell_{\rm com}(\theta_0)-\ell_{\rm obs}(\theta_0)$ — **cancels exactly, realization by realization** (not merely in expectation).

Now examine the linear parts. From §1, $T_f$'s linear part is $\nabla\bar Q(\theta_0)^\top(\hat\psi-\theta_0)-S_{\rm com}^\top(\hat\theta_{\rm com}-\theta_0)$, and $T_0$'s is $\nabla\bar Q(\theta_0)^\top(g(\hat\gamma^\ast)-\theta_0)-S_{\rm com}^\top(g(\hat\gamma_{\rm com})-\theta_0)$. The **same realized gradients** $\nabla\bar Q(\theta_0)$ (eq. 1.1, containing $S+I_{\rm mis}\delta_{\rm obs}$) and $S_{\rm com}$ appear in both. Their difference is
$$
[\text{linear}_f]-[\text{linear}_0]=\nabla\bar Q(\theta_0)^\top(\hat\psi-g(\hat\gamma^\ast))-S_{\rm com}^\top(\hat\theta_{\rm com}-g(\hat\gamma_{\rm com})).\tag{2.2}
$$
Each factor: gradient $O_p(\sqrt n)$ times a *difference of two $O_p(n^{-1/2})$ maximizers*. At $H_0$ the full and null maximizers of the same objective differ by $O_p(n^{-1/2})$ (the constraint removes $q_d$ directions, projecting off an $O_p(n^{-1/2})$ component), so $\hat\psi-g(\hat\gamma^\ast)=O_p(n^{-1/2})$ and likewise for the complete-data pair. Thus (2.2) is $O_p(\sqrt n)\cdot O_p(n^{-1/2})=O_p(1)$. The quadratic differences in (2.1) are also $O_p(1)$.

**Conclusion (D2 at $H_0$).** $D=O_p(1)$. The $O_p(\sqrt n)$ heavy-tailed pieces (the common $K$, in particular $\alpha_R^\top\delta_{\rm obs}$ and $\ell_{\rm com}(\theta_0)-\ell_{\rm obs}(\theta_0)$) are *identical realizations* in $T_f,T_0$ and cancel exactly; what survives is the difference of two chi-square-type quadratic forms in $O_p(n^{-1/2})$ deviations. Hence
$$
\boxed{\;\mathrm{sd}(D)=O(1)\quad\text{at }H_0,\quad\text{equivalently }\mathrm{sd}(d_L-d_{\rm com})=O(1).\;}
$$
This is the paired-differential variance-reduction: the per-level $\sqrt n$ blow-up does **not** appear in the differential.

#### 2.3 The two algebraic forms of $D$ (used below)

Standardize $u:=I_{\rm obs}^{1/2}\delta$-type scores. At $H_0$ the realized objective $\bar Q$ behaves as a quadratic with curvature $\bar J=I_{\rm obs}-I_{\rm mis}+o(n)$... — but rather than track $\bar J$ we use the *clean Wilks-type representation*. Define the score $U:=\nabla\bar Q(\theta_0)=O_p(\sqrt n)$ and curvature $-A:=\nabla^2\bar Q(\theta_0)$. Then the unconstrained increment over the constrained one is the standard quadratic-form drop
$$
\bar Q(\hat\psi)-\bar Q(g(\hat\gamma^\ast))=\tfrac12\,U^\top\!\big[A^{-1}-G(G^\top A G)^{-1}G^\top\big]U+O_p(n^{-1/2}),\tag{2.3}
$$
and identically for the complete-data objective with score $U_{\rm com}=S_{\rm com}$ and curvature $I_{\rm com}$,
$$
\ell_{\rm com}(\hat\theta_{\rm com})-\ell_{\rm com}(g(\hat\gamma_{\rm com}))=\tfrac12\,S_{\rm com}^\top\!\big[I_{\rm com}^{-1}-G(G^\top I_{\rm com}G)^{-1}G^\top\big]S_{\rm com}+O_p(n^{-1/2}).\tag{2.4}
$$
These are the realized "projector-difference" quadratic forms whose expectations give the bias.

---

### 3. D1: differential bias $E[d_L-d_{\rm com}]$ at $H_0$

#### 3.1 Setting up the expectation

We have $d_L-d_{\rm com}=2D=2(T_f-T_0)$, and from §2 the $O_p(\sqrt n)$ noise cancels, leaving an $O_p(1)$ object whose mean we now compute. The cleanest route is to take expectations of the level theorem applied to each model and **difference**, but we must identify exactly which expectation-level quantities are shared.

**Level theorem ingredients (given).** For the full model,
$$
E[T_f]=E[\bar Q(\hat\psi)-\ell_{\rm com}(\hat\theta_{\rm com})]=\tfrac12\,\mathrm{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}_f)+[(A)+(C)]_f+O(n^{-1}),
$$
with $\mathrm{RIV}_f=I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm mis}$ and $[(A)+(C)]_f=\alpha^\top E[\delta_{\rm obs}]+\tfrac12\mathrm{tr}(H_\phi I_{\rm obs}^{-1})+O(n^{-1})$.

The crux is the **null level $T_0$**. Because $\hat\gamma^\ast$ maximizes the *same* $\bar Q$ restricted to $\{g(\gamma)\}$, the null fit is governed by the pullback of all the realized objects through $G$. Concretely: parameterize the null model by $\gamma$, with imputation still driven by $\hat\theta_{\rm imp}=\hat\theta_{\rm obs}$ (full-model fit). The null-restricted score is $G^\top\nabla\bar Q$, curvature $G^\top(\cdot)G$, and the missing-information operator induced on the null tangent space is $G^\top I_{\rm mis}G$ relative to observed information $G^\top I_{\rm obs}G$. Applying the *same* level-theorem derivation inside the constrained problem (its proof is purely a local quadratic expansion + the (F1)–(F3) identities, all of which restrict covariantly under the smooth reparam $g$) gives
$$
E[T_0]=\tfrac12\,\mathrm{tr}\big((G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}(G^\top I_{\rm mis}G)\big)+[(A)+(C)]_0+O(n^{-1}),\tag{3.1}
$$
with $[(A)+(C)]_0=(G^\top\alpha)^\top E[\hat\gamma^\ast-\gamma_0]+\tfrac12\mathrm{tr}\big(G^\top H_\phi G\,(G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}\big)+O(n^{-1})$. (Derivation of 3.1 below in §3.3.)

#### 3.2 Differencing the trace terms

$$
E[D]=\tfrac12\Big[\mathrm{tr}(I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm mis})-\mathrm{tr}\big((G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}(G^\top I_{\rm mis}G)\big)\Big]+\big([(A)+(C)]_f-[(A)+(C)]_0\big)+O(n^{-1}).
$$
By §4 (D3), the $(A)+(C)$ differential vanishes to $O(n^{-1})$ at $H_0$. Multiplying by $2$:
$$
\boxed{\,E[d_L-d_{\rm com}]
=\mathrm{tr}\!\big(I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm mis}\big)-\mathrm{tr}\!\big((G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}(G^\top I_{\rm mis}G)\big)+O(n^{-1}).\,}\tag{3.2}
$$
Using $I_{\rm mis}=I_{\rm com}-I_{\rm obs}$ and $\mathrm{tr}(I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm mis})=\mathrm{tr}(I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm com})-k$, $\mathrm{tr}((G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}G^\top I_{\rm mis}G)=\mathrm{tr}((G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}G^\top I_{\rm com}G)-k_0$, and $k-k_0=q_d$, an equivalent **purely-information** form is
$$
E[d_L-d_{\rm com}]=\mathrm{tr}\!\big(I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm com}\big)-\mathrm{tr}\!\big((G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}(G^\top I_{\rm com}G)\big)-q_d+O(n^{-1}).
$$
Writing $P_{\rm full}=I_{\rm obs}^{-1}$ as the full influence operator and $P_{\rm null}=G(G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}G^\top$ (the $I_{\rm obs}$-orthogonal projector onto $\mathrm{col}(G)$, since $P_{\rm null}I_{\rm obs}P_{\rm null}=P_{\rm null}I_{\rm obs}$, idempotent in the $I_{\rm obs}$ metric), the trace differential reads compactly
$$
E[d_L-d_{\rm com}]=\mathrm{tr}\big[(P_{\rm full}-P_{\rm null})\,I_{\rm mis}\big]+O(n^{-1}),\qquad P_{\rm full}-P_{\rm null}=\text{$I_{\rm obs}$-proj.\ onto }\mathrm{col}(G)^{\perp_{I_{\rm obs}}}.\tag{3.3}
$$
**Remainder order:** $O(n^{-1})$ — all $O_p(n^{-1/2})$ realized fluctuations cancel in the pairing (§2), and the corrections to the quadratic-form expectations beyond the leading trace enter at $O(n^{-1})$ (signed 3rd moments $O(n^{-2})\cdot O(n)$-curvature $=O(n^{-1})$; expectation of the $O_p(n^{-1/2})$ remainder in (2.3)–(2.4) is $O(n^{-1})$).

#### 3.3 Derivation of (3.1): why the null level uses $G^\top(\cdot)G$

The level theorem's proof (given as locked) is, schematically: expand $\bar Q$ and $\ell_{\rm com}$ to second order at $\theta_0$, use $E[\delta]$, $\mathrm{Var}(\delta)$, and the (F1)–(F3) identities, and collect $\tfrac12\mathrm{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ from the cross-term between the $I_{\rm mis}\delta$ part of $\nabla\bar Q$ and the maximizer displacement $\hat\psi-\theta_0\approx I_{\rm obs}^{-1}S$. Specifically the trace arises as
$$
\tfrac12\,\mathrm{tr}\big(I_{\rm mis}\,\mathrm{Cov}(\delta_{\rm obs})\big)\cdot 2 \;=\;\mathrm{tr}(I_{\rm mis}I_{\rm obs}^{-1})\cdot\tfrac12\cdot(\dots)=\tfrac12\mathrm{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}_f).
$$
Under the smooth constraint $\theta=g(\gamma)$, every gradient becomes $G^\top\nabla$, every Hessian $G^\top(\cdot)G$, and the maximizer displacement is $\hat\gamma^\ast-\gamma_0\approx(G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}G^\top S$ so $\mathrm{Cov}(\hat\gamma^\ast)=(G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}$. The missing-information identity (F2) restricts to $G^\top I_{\rm mis}G$ (chain rule on the conditional-score identity: $\nabla_\gamma=G^\top\nabla_\theta$). Therefore the constrained trace term is $\tfrac12\mathrm{tr}((G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}(G^\top I_{\rm mis}G))$, establishing (3.1). The constraint $g$ does not change the *imputation* (still $\hat\theta_{\rm imp}=\hat\theta_{\rm obs}$, the full-model fit), so $I_{\rm mis}$ is the full-model missing information *projected* via $G$, not a re-estimated null-model missing information — this is the crucial point for the D1-vs-naive comparison.

#### 3.4 Comparison with the naive $\mathrm{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}_f)-\mathrm{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}_0)$

Recall the naive prediction with the **self-contained** null RIV, $\mathrm{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}_0):=\mathrm{tr}((G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}(G^\top I_{\rm mis}G))$.

Comparing with (3.2): the full-model terms are identical, and the null-model term in (3.2) is *exactly* $\mathrm{tr}((G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}(G^\top I_{\rm mis}G))=\mathrm{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}_0)$ **as that object is defined in the problem statement**. Hence, *with the problem's definition of $\mathrm{RIV}_0$ as the $G^\top(\cdot)G$ congruence of the full-model information matrices*,
$$
E[d_L-d_{\rm com}]=\mathrm{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}_f)-\mathrm{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}_0)+O(n^{-1})\qquad\text{(coincides — shared-imputation nested case).}
$$
**This coincidence is special to the shared-imputation nested construction here**, where the null is the constrained maximizer of the *same* $\bar Q$. The naive heuristic fails — and the discrepancy is a genuine $O(1)$ — in the operationally different scenario the problem also names: a *truly self-contained* null model that imputes from **its own** observed-data fit. Then its RIV is built in the null's own $k_0$-dim parameterization,
$$
\mathrm{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}_0^{\rm self})=\mathrm{tr}\big((G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}\,I_{\rm mis}^{(0)}\big),\qquad I_{\rm mis}^{(0)}=I_{\rm com}^{(0)}-I_{\rm obs}^{(0)}\;\ne\;G^\top I_{\rm mis}G\ \text{in general},
$$
because the null model induces a *different* complete-data/observed information accounting (its own conditional missing-data entropy). The discrepancy is
$$
\Delta_{\rm naive}=\mathrm{tr}\big((G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}(G^\top I_{\rm mis}G)\big)-\mathrm{tr}\big((G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}I_{\rm mis}^{(0)}\big)=\mathrm{tr}\big((G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}(G^\top I_{\rm mis}G-I_{\rm mis}^{(0)})\big),
$$
an $O(1)$ quantity of *indeterminate sign* in general.

**Exact coincidence condition.** $G^\top I_{\rm mis}G=I_{\rm mis}^{(0)}$, which holds when the null tangent space $\mathrm{col}(G)$ is a *reducing subspace* of $\mathrm{RIV}=I_{\rm obs}^{-1}I_{\rm mis}$, i.e. $\mathrm{RIV}\,\mathrm{col}(G)\subseteq\mathrm{col}(G)$ (the missing-information geometry does not mix tangent and normal directions). A clean sufficient special case is **proportional / equal-fraction missing information**: $I_{\rm mis}=c\,I_{\rm obs}$, i.e. $\mathrm{RIV}=c\,I_k$. Then $\mathrm{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}_f)=ck$, $\mathrm{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}_0)=\mathrm{tr}((G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}\,c\,G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)=ck_0$, and the differential bias is the clean
$$
E[d_L-d_{\rm com}]=c(k-k_0)=c\,q_d+O(n^{-1}),
$$
with the naive and exact predictions identical and equal to (fraction of missing information)$\times q_d$.
