Off this invariant case, $\mathrm{tr}((G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}G^\top I_{\rm mis}G)\ne$ any single fixed multiple of $q_d$, and the trace differential (3.3) — a difference of RIV-trace functionals over the nested $I_{\rm obs}$-projectors $P_{\rm full}\succeq P_{\rm null}$ — is the correct object.

**Characterization of the general discrepancy.** $P_{\rm full}-P_{\rm null}$ is the $I_{\rm obs}$-orthogonal projector onto the $q_d$-dim complement of $\mathrm{col}(G)$. The differential bias (3.3) is therefore $\mathrm{tr}$ of $I_{\rm mis}$ "seen through" exactly the directions the alternative adds. It is $\ge 0$ when $I_{\rm mis}\succeq 0$ (always, missing information is PSD) — so the MI deviance numerator is **inflated** relative to complete data, the inflation equal to the missing-information content of the $q_d$ extra directions, in the $I_{\rm obs}$ metric. The naive self-contained difference can over- or under-state this depending on whether the null model's own missing-information operator $I_{\rm mis}^{(0)}$ exceeds or falls short of the projected $G^\top I_{\rm mis}G$ on $\mathrm{col}(G)$.

---

### 4. D3: the $(A)+(C)$ differential

#### 4.1 At $H_0$

The $(A)+(C)$ term of a level is $\alpha^\top E[\delta]+\tfrac12\mathrm{tr}(H_\phi\,\mathrm{Var}(\delta))$, arising entirely from the $F(\theta_0,\hat\theta_{\rm imp})$ side (the realized imputation-entropy curvature in $\phi$), via $F(\theta_0,\hat\theta_{\rm imp})=F(\theta_0,\theta_0)+\alpha_R^\top\delta_{\rm obs}+\tfrac12\delta_{\rm obs}^\top H_R\delta_{\rm obs}+\cdots$, taking expectations with $E[\alpha_R]=\alpha$, $E[H_R]=H_\phi$, $E[\delta_{\rm obs}]=O(n^{-1})$, $\mathrm{Var}(\delta_{\rm obs})=I_{\rm obs}^{-1}$.

**These pieces depend only on the imputation $\hat\theta_{\rm imp}=\hat\theta_{\rm obs}$ and the observed-data $\delta_{\rm obs}$ — both shared by the two fits — and not on which model's quadratic was maximized.** In the realized decomposition (§2.1), $\alpha_R^\top\delta_{\rm obs}$ and $\tfrac12\delta_{\rm obs}^\top H_R\delta_{\rm obs}$ are part of the **common base constant $K$** (they sit inside $F(\theta_0,\hat\theta_{\rm imp})\subset\bar Q(\theta_0)$, which is identical in $T_f$ and $T_0$). They cancel **exactly per realization** in $D$, hence in expectation. Therefore
$$
[(A)+(C)]_f-[(A)+(C)]_0=0\quad\text{exactly to }O(n^{-1})\text{ at }H_0.
$$
More carefully through (3.1): the null model's $(A)+(C)$ as written there uses $E[\hat\gamma^\ast-\gamma_0]$ and $\mathrm{Var}$ on the null tangent space. But the $(A)+(C)$ content lives in $K$, which is *parameter-fit-independent*; the only place a model-specific $\delta$ could enter is the *cross* between $\alpha$ and the maximizer displacement. At $H_0$, $E[\delta_f]=O(n^{-1})$ and $E[\hat\gamma^\ast-\gamma_0]$ maps to $G\,O(n^{-1})$, and these displacements differ by $O(n^{-1})$. Hence the residual differential is
$$
[(A)+(C)]_f-[(A)+(C)]_0=\alpha^\top\big(E[\delta_f]-G\,E[\hat\gamma^\ast-\gamma_0]\big)+O(n^{-1})=O(n)\cdot O(n^{-1})\cdot\!\!\underbrace{}_{\text{but in }K\text{ it is identical}}\!\!=O(n^{-1}).
$$
The cleanest statement: because the $(A)+(C)$ content is entirely inside the common $K$, it cancels exactly; the *first nonvanishing* differential is the $O(n^{-1})$ residue from model-specific second-order ($E[\delta]$) bias differences.
$$
\boxed{[(A)+(C)]\text{ differential}=O(n^{-1})\text{ at }H_0\ (\text{exact zero to }O(1)\text{ and to }O(n^{-1/2})).}
$$

#### 4.2 Under local alternatives $\theta_n=g(\gamma_0)+h/\sqrt n$, $h\notin\mathrm{col}(G)$

Now the constrained fit cannot follow the $h$-shift in the $q_d$ directions outside $\mathrm{col}(G)$. The full fit centers at $\theta_n$; the null fit centers at its $I_{\rm obs}$-projection $g(\gamma_0)+P_{\rm null}h/\sqrt n$. The mean displacements differ by
$$
E[\delta_f]-G\,E[\hat\gamma^\ast-\gamma_0]=(I_k-P_{\rm null}I_{\rm obs})\,h/\sqrt n+O(n^{-1})=O(n^{-1/2}),
$$
the surviving $h$-component being $O(n^{-1/2})$ (an $O(1)$ vector scaled by $n^{-1/2}$). The $(A)$-type differential is $\alpha^\top(\text{that})$. Here $\alpha=O(n)$ *as a total* (it is $\sum_i$ of per-unit $O(1)$ contributions, $\alpha=n\bar a$, $\bar a=O(1)$, on covariance coords). But the relevant contraction is the *per-datum normalized* one: the $(A)+(C)$ in the level theorem is genuinely $O(1)$ because $\alpha^\top E[\delta]=O(n)\cdot O(n^{-1})=O(1)$. Under the local shift, the *difference* of $E[\delta]$ between models is $O(n^{-1/2})$ rather than the $O(n^{-1})$ of $H_0$, so
$$
[(A)+(C)]_f-[(A)+(C)]_0=\alpha^\top\big(E[\delta_f]-G E[\hat\gamma^\ast-\gamma_0]\big)+O(n^{-1})=O(n)\cdot O(n^{-1/2})\cdot\tfrac1n\Big|_{\text{normalized}}=O(n^{-1/2})=o(1).
$$
(The normalization: $\alpha^\top\delta$ is $O(1)$ when $\delta=O(n^{-1})$; replacing $\delta$-*difference* $O(n^{-1})\to O(n^{-1/2})$ rescales by $n^{1/2}$ only if $\alpha$ stayed fixed — but the level-theorem $(A)+(C)=O(1)$ already encodes $\alpha\cdot E[\delta]=O(1)$ with $E[\delta]=O(n^{-1})$; the local shift changes the *cross* mean-difference from $O(n^{-1})$ to $O(n^{-1/2})$, i.e. multiplies the $O(1)$ baseline scale by $n^{-1/2}\!\cdot\! n =\dots$). Carrying the bookkeeping precisely: baseline $\alpha^\top E[\delta]=O(1)$ uses $\|\alpha\|=O(n)$, $\|E[\delta]\|=O(n^{-1})$. The differential uses $\|\alpha\|=O(n)$, $\|E[\delta_f]-GE[\hat\gamma^\ast]\|=O(n^{-1/2})$ **but only the $H_0$-cancelled part is removed**; the surviving local piece is $P^\perp h/\sqrt n$ contracted with the *per-unit* intensity $\bar a=O(1)$, giving $\bar a^\top P^\perp h/\sqrt n\cdot$(count factor). The count factor for the *differential* is $O(1)$ (not $O(n)$) because the $O(n)$ accumulation already cancelled in $K$; thus the differential is $O(n^{-1/2})$.) Therefore
$$
\boxed{[(A)+(C)]\text{ differential}=O(n^{-1/2})=o(1)\text{ under local alternatives.}}
$$
**Consequence:** the information-trace differential $\mathrm{tr}[(P_{\rm full}-P_{\rm null})I_{\rm mis}]=O(1)$ **dominates** local-power distortion; the $(A)+(C)$ differential is asymptotically negligible. The robustness of the *paired* statistic is precisely this: the $O(1)$, heavy, design-imbalance $(A)+(C)$ term is common-mode and drops out, leaving the clean trace differential.

#### 4.3 At a fixed alternative (pseudo-truths $O(1)$ apart, non-nested)

Now the two models' pseudo-true values $\theta_f^\star,\theta_0^\star$ are $O(1)$ apart; imputations, $\alpha$, $H_\phi$, $I_{\rm obs}$, $I_{\rm mis}$, $E[\delta]$ all become **model-specific and non-shared** — the base constant $K$ is no longer common (each level expands around a different base point). Then:
- (D2) The exact $O_p(\sqrt n)$ cancellation breaks: $T_f,T_0$ are increments along $O_p(1)$-separated, non-shared segments, the $\alpha_R^\top\delta$ heavy-tailed noise no longer subtracts, and
$$
\boxed{\mathrm{sd}(D)=O(\sqrt n)\text{ at a fixed alternative.}}
$$
- (D3) The $(A)+(C)$ differential is genuinely $\boxed{O(1)}$ and does not cancel; both it and the trace differential are $O(1)$ and model-specific. This is the regime where MI distorts non-nested model ranking through *both* channels (the model-specific $\mathrm{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ and the model-specific design-imbalance $(A)+(C)$), consistent with the IC application's "high-RIV models look artificially better."

---

### 5. Summary of orders

| Quantity | $H_0$ | Local ($h/\sqrt n$) | Fixed alt. |
|---|---|---|---|
| $E[d_L-d_{\rm com}]$ (bias) | $\mathrm{tr}[(P_{\rm full}-P_{\rm null})I_{\rm mis}]=O(1)$ | $O(1)$ (same leading trace + noncentrality shift) | $O(1)$, model-specific |
| remainder | $O(n^{-1})$ | $O(n^{-1/2})$ | $O(1)$ |
| $\mathrm{sd}(D)$ | $O(1)$ | $O(1)$ | $O(\sqrt n)$ |
| $(A)+(C)$ differential | $O(n^{-1})$ | $O(n^{-1/2})=o(1)$ | $O(1)$ |

**Where each input was used.** (F1) gave $\nabla_\psi F(\theta_0,\theta_0)=0$, so the only $F$-gradient at $\theta_0$ is the $I_{\rm mis}\delta_{\rm obs}$ second-argument term (eq. 1.1) — this is what makes the $\sqrt n$ noise reside in the *common base constant $K$* rather than in a model-specific gradient. (F2) supplied the $I_{\rm mis}$ (and $G^\top I_{\rm mis}G$) appearing in the trace differential (3.2)–(3.3). (F3) supplied $\alpha,H_\phi$ that constitute $(A)+(C)$, shown to be common-mode and to cancel (D3). MLE moment orders ($E[\delta]=O(n^{-1})$, $\mathrm{Var}=I_{\rm obs}^{-1}=O(n^{-1})$, 3rd/4th moments $O(n^{-2})$) fixed every remainder: the trace bias at $O(1)$, its remainder at $O(n^{-1})$, and the $(A)+(C)$ residues. The projection identity $P_{\rm null}=G(G^\top I_{\rm obs}G)^{-1}G^\top$ (idempotent in the $I_{\rm obs}$ metric) turned the difference of constrained/unconstrained quadratic forms into the clean $(P_{\rm full}-P_{\rm null})$ projector form and exposed the RIV-invariant-subspace coincidence condition versus the naive $\mathrm{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}_f)-\mathrm{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}_0)$.

Numerically (k=3,k0=2): correct=0.6051 vs derivation's=0.6136, a real difference. Over 2000 random draws the derivation OVER-states with FIXED sign (frac>0 = 1.000). The two coincide exactly iff col(G) is RIV-invariant (axis-aligned test: diff=1e-16), including the proportional-info case I_mis=cI_obs where both give c*q_d (confirmed numerically =0.3 for c=0.3,q_d=1).

(7) D2 noise orders (O(1)/O(1)/O(sqrt n)) and D3 ((A)+(C) differential O(n^{-1})/O(n^{-1/2})/O(1)): I confirmed all of these are CORRECT; their mechanism (common K cancels the O_p(sqrt n) alpha_R^T delta heavy tail realization-by-realization) is metric-independent and sound.

issues.description: WRONG METRIC IN THE D1 HEADLINE FORMULA (the central deliverable). The differential bias requires the constrained MI maximizer gamma-hat* to project the score in the metric A = -grad^2 Qbar(theta0) = I_obs + I_mis = I_com (the COMPLETE-data information), not I_obs. The derivation correctly keeps A abstract in eq (2.3), but in sec 3.3 it silently sets the Qbar curvature to I_obs, asserting 'gamma-hat* - gamma0 approx (G^T I_obs G)^{-1} G^T S, Cov(gamma-hat*)=(G^T I_obs G)^{-1}'. This is exactly the conflation of the constrained-MI-maximizer with the null model's OWN observed-data MLE (which is what would project in the I_obs metric). The correct curvature is I_com, verified three ways: (i) Bartlett conditional-info identity E[-grad_psi^2 F(theta0,theta0)]=I_mis so -grad^2 Qbar=I_obs+I_mis=I_com; (ii) Fisher's EM identity; (iii) the FIML self-consistency I_com^{-1}U=delta_obs (the unconstrained MI fit must equal hat_theta_obs). CORRECT formula: E[d_L-d_com]=tr(I_obs^{-1}I_mis) - tr((G^T I_com G)^{-1} G^T I_com I_obs^{-1} I_mis G) + O(n^{-1}). The derivation's boxed (3.2)/(3.3) tr[(I_obs^{-1}-G(G^T I_obs G)^{-1}G^T)I_mis] is wrong: the null term uses I_obs where it must use I_com. The full-model term tr(RIV_f) happens to be correct because A^{-1}=I_com^{-1} contracted with Cov(U)-I_com=I_com I_obs^{-1}I_mis yields tr(I_obs^{-1}I_mis) cleanly. Numerically the error is O(1) (0.6136 vs 0.6051 in a 3x2 example) with a systematically positive sign (derivation over-states across 2000 random draws). The clean 'I_obs-orthogonal projector P_full-P_null onto col(G)^perp' geometry and the formula tr[(P_full-P_null)I_mis] are therefore incorrect except in the special proportional/RIV-invariant case.

D1 NAIVE-COMPARISON CONCLUSION IS COMPROMISED BY THE SAME ERROR. Sec 3.4 / the headline conclude that the correct formula COINCIDES exactly with the naive tr(RIV_f)-tr(RIV_0) in this shared-imputation nested construction (since both use the null term tr((G^T I_obs G)^{-1} G^T I_mis G)). But that coincidence only holds because BOTH the derivation's formula and the problem's naive RIV_0 are in the wrong I_obs metric. The CORRECT differential uses the I_com-metric null term and therefore does NOT coincide with the naive tr(RIV_f)-tr(RIV_0) in general either. The genuine discrepancy is thus algebraic/metric (I_com vs I_obs in the null projection), present already in the shared-imputation construction the paper foregrounds, not merely the conceptual self-contained-imputation distinction the derivation emphasizes. The derivation's framing ('they coincide; naive fails only for self-contained imputation') is therefore wrong. The exact coincidence condition the derivation states (col(G) is RIV-invariant, RIV*col(G) subset col(G)) IS in fact the correct boundary at which derivation=truth (axis-aligned numerical test gives exact agreement), including the proportional-info case I_mis=cI_obs giving c*q_d -- but the derivation reaches this condition for the wrong reason (comparing two same-metric expressions) rather than as the condition under which the I_obs and I_com metrics agree on col(G).

D3 local-alternative bookkeeping (sec 4.2) is garbled. The text writes several mutually inconsistent scaling chains for the (A)+(C) differential ('O(n)*O(n^{-1/2})*(1/sqrt n)', 'O(n^{1/2})*O(n^{-1/2})', 'multiplies the O(1) baseline scale by n^{-1/2}*n=...') with a visibly incomplete clause. The final answer O(n^{-1/2})=o(1) is plausible and the qualitative conclusion (the O(1) trace differential dominates local power) is correct, but the order is asserted through hand-waving rather than a clean derivation; the count-factor argument ('O(1) not O(n) because accumulation cancelled in K') is stated but not rigorously established.

Eq (3.1) for E[T_0] inherits the metric error: it writes the constrained trace as (1/2)tr((G^T I_obs G)^{-1}(G^T I_mis G)) and Cov(gamma-hat*)=(G^T I_obs G)^{-1}. By the same A=I_com argument the constrained level should be (1/2)tr((G^T I_com G)^{-1} G^T I_com I_obs^{-1} I_mis G). Note the full-model E[T_f]=(1/2)tr(RIV_f) in sec 3.1 is nonetheless correct.

referee.notes:
VERDICT: unsound, due to a single load-bearing error that propagates into the two D1 headline claims.

WHAT IS WRONG: The D1 differential-bias formula (the paper's central deliverable) uses the wrong projection metric for the constrained MI maximizer. The constrained maximizer gamma-hat* of the shared Qbar projects the score in the Qbar-curvature metric A = -grad^2 Qbar = I_com (complete-data information), NOT in I_obs. The derivation correctly carries A abstractly through eq (2.3)-(2.4) but then in sec 3.3 silently replaces the Qbar curvature with I_obs -- precisely the warned-against conflation of the constrained-MI-maximizer with the null model's own observed-data MLE. Correct null term: tr((G^T I_com G)^{-1} G^T I_com I_obs^{-1} I_mis G); the derivation's is tr((G^T I_obs G)^{-1} G^T I_mis G). These differ at O(1) with the derivation systematically over-stating (fixed positive sign over 2000 random draws). The full-model term tr(RIV_f) is correct. I validated my machinery by independently reproducing the LOCKED full-model level theorem +1/2 tr(RIV_f) exactly.

WHAT IS RIGHT (and worth preserving): (i) the realized-pairing strategy and the identification that the heavy-tailed O_p(sqrt n) noise (alpha_R^T delta_obs and l_com-l_obs) lives in a COMMON base constant K that cancels realization-by-realization -- this is metric-independent and correct; (ii) ALL of D2 (sd(D)=O(1) at H0 and local, O(sqrt n) at fixed alt) is correct; (iii) ALL of D3 order conclusions ((A)+(C) differential O(n^{-1}) at H0, o(1) local, O(1) fixed) are correct, with the at-H0 exact-cancellation via common K being the cleanest and most robust part of the paper; (iv) the RIV-invariance coincidence condition and the proportional-info special case (c*q_d) are numerically correct -- in fact RIV-invariance of col(G) is exactly the boundary at which the (corrected) formula and the naive prediction agree, because that is when the I_obs and I_com metrics agree on col(G).

FIX: Substitute A=I_com into eqs (2.3),(3.1),(3.2),(3.3) and sec 3.4. The full-model piece tr(RIV_f) survives; the null piece becomes the I_com-metric trace above. The clean 'I_obs-orthogonal projector onto col(G)^perp' geometry must be replaced by the I_com-metric projector (with the extra I_com I_obs^{-1} I_mis weighting), and the 'coincides with naive in the nested case' claim must be retracted -- the discrepancy is already present in the shared-imputation construction. D2 and D3 need no change.

One caveat on my own analysis: I assumed the standard EM/MI result -grad^2(Q-function)=I_com applies to this self-imputed Qbar; the FIML-consistency cross-check (hat_psi=hat_theta_obs) independently forces A=I_com, so this is not merely an assumption. If the paper intends a different (e.g. observed-information) convention for the curvature, that convention would itself be inconsistent with hat_psi=hat_theta_obs and would need flagging.
